subject: Mathematics
question_html: If the function f defined as f(x) = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{cc} \frac{\text x^{2}-9}{\text x-3} & , \quad \text x \neq 3 \\ \text k & , \quad \text x=3 \end{array}\right.{/tex} is continuous at x = 3, find the value of k.
question_text: If the function f defined as f(x) = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{cc} \frac{\text x^{2}-9}{\text x-3} & , \quad \text x \neq 3 \\ \text k & , \quad \text x=3 \end{array}\right.{/tex} is continuous at x = 3, find the value of k.
explanation: Since f(x ) is continuous at x = 3. {tex}\therefore \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3} f(x)=f(3){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3} f(x)=k{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} f(3) = k] {tex}\Rightarrow \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3} \frac{x^{2}-9}{x-3}=k {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3} \frac{(x-3)(x+3)}{x-3} = k{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3}(x+3)=k {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 6=k{/tex} Thus, f(x) is continuous at x = 3. if k = 6.
solution: Since f(x ) is continuous at x = 3. {tex}\therefore \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3} f(x)=f(3){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3} f(x)=k{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} f(3) = k] {tex}\Rightarrow \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3} \frac{x^{2}-9}{x-3}=k {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3} \frac{(x-3)(x+3)}{x-3} = k{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3}(x+3)=k {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 6=k{/tex} Thus, f(x) is continuous at x = 3. if k = 6.
solution_html:
Since f(x ) is continuous at x = 3.
{tex}\therefore \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3} f(x)=f(3){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3} f(x)=k{/tex}    [{tex}\because{/tex} f(3) = k]
{tex}\Rightarrow \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3} \frac{x^{2}-9}{x-3}=k {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3} \frac{(x-3)(x+3)}{x-3} = k{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \lim _\limits{x \rightarrow 3}(x+3)=k {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 6=k{/tex}
Thus, f(x) is continuous at x = 3. if k = 6.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the value of {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} at {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex}, x = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} - cos 2{tex}\theta{/tex}, y = sin {tex}\theta{/tex} - sin 2{tex}\theta{/tex}.
question_text: Find the value of {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} at {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex} , x = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} - cos 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} , y = sin {tex}\theta{/tex} - sin 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\frac{d x}{d \theta}{/tex} = -sin {tex}\theta{/tex} + 2 sin 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d \theta}{/tex} = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} - 2 cos 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\cos \theta-2 \cos 2 \theta}{-\sin \theta+2 \sin 2 \theta}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\left.\frac{d y}{d x}\right|_{\theta=\frac{\pi}{3}}=\sqrt{3}{/tex}
solution: {tex}\frac{d x}{d \theta}{/tex} = -sin {tex}\theta{/tex} + 2 sin 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d \theta}{/tex} = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} - 2 cos 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\cos \theta-2 \cos 2 \theta}{-\sin \theta+2 \sin 2 \theta}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\left.\frac{d y}{d x}\right|_{\theta=\frac{\pi}{3}}=\sqrt{3}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\frac{d x}{d \theta}{/tex} = -sin{tex}\theta{/tex} + 2 sin 2{tex}\theta{/tex}
{tex}\frac{d y}{d \theta}{/tex} = cos{tex}\theta{/tex} - 2 cos 2{tex}\theta{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\cos \theta-2 \cos 2 \theta}{-\sin \theta+2 \sin 2 \theta}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\left.\frac{d y}{d x}\right|_{\theta=\frac{\pi}{3}}=\sqrt{3}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the derivative of xlogx w.r.t. logx.
question_text: Find the derivative of x logx w.r.t. logx.
explanation: Let u = x logx and v = log x Now, log u = (log x) 2 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{u} \frac{d u}{d x}=2 \log x \cdot \frac{1}{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=\frac{2 \log x}{x} x^{\log x}{/tex} Again, v = log x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{1}{x}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d u}{d v}=2 x^{\log x} \log x{/tex}
solution: Let u = x logx and v = log x Now, log u = (log x) 2 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{u} \frac{d u}{d x}=2 \log x \cdot \frac{1}{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=\frac{2 \log x}{x} x^{\log x}{/tex} Again, v = log x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{1}{x}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d u}{d v}=2 x^{\log x} \log x{/tex}
solution_html:
Let u = xlogx and v = log x
Now, log u = (log x)2
 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{u} \frac{d u}{d x}=2 \log x \cdot \frac{1}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=\frac{2 \log x}{x} x^{\log x}{/tex}
Again, v = log x
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{1}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{d u}{d v}=2 x^{\log x} \log x{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+x}+\sqrt{1-x}}{2}\right){/tex}, that show that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-1}{2 \sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+x}+\sqrt{1-x}}{2}\right){/tex} , that show that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-1}{2 \sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex} .
explanation: To prove: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-1}{2 \sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex} Given: {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+x}+\sqrt{1-x}}{2}\right){/tex} Put x = cos2 {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{1+\cos 2 \theta}+\sqrt{1-\cos 2 \theta}}{2}\right]{/tex} {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{1+2 \cos ^2 \theta-1}+\sqrt{1-\left(1-2 \sin ^2 \theta\right)}}{2}\right]{/tex} [cos 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} = 2cos 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} - 1 = 1 - 2sin 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} ] {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{2} \cos \theta+\sqrt{2} \sin \theta}{2}\right]{/tex} {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \cos \theta+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \sin \theta\right]{/tex} {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\sin \frac{\pi}{4} \cos \theta+\cos \frac{\pi}{4} \sin \theta\right]{/tex} [Using sin A cos B + cos A sin B = sin (A + B)] {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{4}+\theta\right)\right]{/tex} {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{4}+\theta{/tex} Put {tex}\theta=\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x{/tex} [x = cos 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} ] {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x{/tex} Differentiate above equation w.r.t x: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=0+\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-1}{2 \sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex}
solution: To prove: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-1}{2 \sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex} Given: {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+x}+\sqrt{1-x}}{2}\right){/tex} Put x = cos2 {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{1+\cos 2 \theta}+\sqrt{1-\cos 2 \theta}}{2}\right]{/tex} {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{1+2 \cos ^2 \theta-1}+\sqrt{1-\left(1-2 \sin ^2 \theta\right)}}{2}\right]{/tex} [cos 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} = 2cos 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} - 1 = 1 - 2sin 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} ] {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{2} \cos \theta+\sqrt{2} \sin \theta}{2}\right]{/tex} {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \cos \theta+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \sin \theta\right]{/tex} {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\sin \frac{\pi}{4} \cos \theta+\cos \frac{\pi}{4} \sin \theta\right]{/tex} [Using sin A cos B + cos A sin B = sin (A + B)] {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{4}+\theta\right)\right]{/tex} {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{4}+\theta{/tex} Put {tex}\theta=\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x{/tex} [x = cos 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} ] {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x{/tex} Differentiate above equation w.r.t x: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=0+\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-1}{2 \sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex}
solution_html:
To prove: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-1}{2 \sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex}
Given: {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+x}+\sqrt{1-x}}{2}\right){/tex}
Put x = cos2{tex}\theta{/tex}
{tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{1+\cos 2 \theta}+\sqrt{1-\cos 2 \theta}}{2}\right]{/tex}
{tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{1+2 \cos ^2 \theta-1}+\sqrt{1-\left(1-2 \sin ^2 \theta\right)}}{2}\right]{/tex} [cos 2{tex}\theta{/tex} = 2cos2{tex}\theta{/tex} - 1 = 1 - 2sin2{tex}\theta{/tex}]
{tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{2} \cos \theta+\sqrt{2} \sin \theta}{2}\right]{/tex}
{tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \cos \theta+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \sin \theta\right]{/tex}
{tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\sin \frac{\pi}{4} \cos \theta+\cos \frac{\pi}{4} \sin \theta\right]{/tex}
[Using sin A cos B + cos A sin B = sin (A + B)]
{tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left[\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{4}+\theta\right)\right]{/tex}
{tex}y=\frac{\pi}{4}+\theta{/tex}
Put {tex}\theta=\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x{/tex} [x = cos 2{tex}\theta{/tex}]
{tex}y=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x{/tex}
Differentiate above equation w.r.t x:
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=0+\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-1}{2 \sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex}

question_html: If y = (cos x)x + tan-1 {tex}\sqrt{\text x}{/tex}, find {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex}.
question_text: If y = (cos x) x + tan -1 {tex}\sqrt{\text x}{/tex} , find {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} .
explanation: y = (cos x) x + tan -1 {tex}\sqrt{\text x}{/tex} = u + v, {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}+\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} log u = x log (cos x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = (cos x) x [-x tan x + log (cos x)] and {tex}\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{1+\text x} \cdot \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{\text x}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = (cos x) x [-x tan x + log (cos x)] + {tex}\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{\text x}(1+\text x)}{/tex}
solution: y = (cos x) x + tan -1 {tex}\sqrt{\text x}{/tex} = u + v, {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}+\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} log u = x log (cos x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = (cos x) x [-x tan x + log (cos x)] and {tex}\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{1+\text x} \cdot \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{\text x}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = (cos x) x [-x tan x + log (cos x)] + {tex}\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{\text x}(1+\text x)}{/tex}
solution_html:
y = (cos x)x + tan-1 {tex}\sqrt{\text x}{/tex} = u + v, {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}+\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex}
log u = x log (cos x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = (cos x)x [-x tan x + log (cos x)]
and {tex}\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{1+\text x} \cdot \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{\text x}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = (cos x)x [-x tan x + log (cos x)] + {tex}\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{\text x}(1+\text x)}{/tex}

question_html: If {tex}y^{2} \cos \left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=a^{2}{/tex}, then find {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}y^{2} \cos \left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=a^{2}{/tex} , then find {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} .
explanation: y 2 cos {tex}\left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right){/tex} = a 2 Then {tex}2\text y\cdot \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}} \cdot \cos \left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right)-\text y^2{/tex} sin {tex}\left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right)\left(-\frac{1}{\text x^2}\right){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}2 \text y \cdot \cos \left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right) \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\text y^2}{\text x^2} \sin \left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right){/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=-\frac{\text y}{2\text x^2} \tan \left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right){/tex}
solution: y 2 cos {tex}\left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right){/tex} = a 2 Then {tex}2\text y\cdot \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}} \cdot \cos \left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right)-\text y^2{/tex} sin {tex}\left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right)\left(-\frac{1}{\text x^2}\right){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}2 \text y \cdot \cos \left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right) \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\text y^2}{\text x^2} \sin \left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right){/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=-\frac{\text y}{2\text x^2} \tan \left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right){/tex}
solution_html:
y2 cos{tex}\left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right){/tex} = a2
Then {tex}2\text y\cdot \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}} \cdot \cos \left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right)-\text y^2{/tex} sin{tex}\left(\frac{1}{\text x}\right)\left(-\frac{1}{\text x^2}\right){/tex} = 0

question_text: If x = a ( {tex}\theta{/tex} - sin {tex}\theta{/tex} ), y = a (1 - cos {tex}\theta{/tex} ), a > 0, then find {tex}\frac{\text d^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}{/tex} at {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac \pi 3{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text d \theta}{/tex} = a sin {tex}\theta{/tex} , {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text d \theta}{/tex} = a(1 - cos {tex}\theta{/tex} ) {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{\sin \theta}{1-\cos \theta}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 \sin \frac{\theta}{2} \cos \frac{\theta}{2}}{2 \sin ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}}=\cot \frac{\theta}{2}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text d^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}=-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \operatorname{cosec}^2 \frac{\theta}{2} \cdot \frac{\text d \theta}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\operatorname{cosec}^2 \frac{\theta}{2}}{2 a(1-\cos \theta)}{/tex} {tex}\left.\frac{\text d^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}\right]_{\theta=\frac{\pi}{3}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{4}{a\left(1-\frac{1}{2}\right)}=-\frac{4}{a}{/tex}
solution: {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text d \theta}{/tex} = a sin {tex}\theta{/tex} , {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text d \theta}{/tex} = a(1 - cos {tex}\theta{/tex} ) {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{\sin \theta}{1-\cos \theta}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 \sin \frac{\theta}{2} \cos \frac{\theta}{2}}{2 \sin ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}}=\cot \frac{\theta}{2}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text d^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}=-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \operatorname{cosec}^2 \frac{\theta}{2} \cdot \frac{\text d \theta}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\operatorname{cosec}^2 \frac{\theta}{2}}{2 a(1-\cos \theta)}{/tex} {tex}\left.\frac{\text d^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}\right]_{\theta=\frac{\pi}{3}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{4}{a\left(1-\frac{1}{2}\right)}=-\frac{4}{a}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text d \theta}{/tex} = a sin {tex}\theta{/tex}, {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text d \theta}{/tex} = a(1 - cos {tex}\theta{/tex})
{tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{\sin \theta}{1-\cos \theta}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 \sin \frac{\theta}{2} \cos \frac{\theta}{2}}{2 \sin ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}}=\cot \frac{\theta}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\text d^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}=-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \operatorname{cosec}^2 \frac{\theta}{2} \cdot \frac{\text d \theta}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\operatorname{cosec}^2 \frac{\theta}{2}}{2 a(1-\cos \theta)}{/tex}
{tex}\left.\frac{\text d^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}\right]_{\theta=\frac{\pi}{3}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{4}{a\left(1-\frac{1}{2}\right)}=-\frac{4}{a}{/tex}

question_html: Find the derivative of xlog x w.r.t. log x.
question_text: Find the derivative of x log x w.r.t. log x.
explanation: Let u = x log x and v = log x Now, log u = (log x) 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\text u} \frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = 2 log {tex}\text x \cdot \frac{1}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{2 \log \text x}{\text x} \cdot \text x^{\log \text x}{/tex} Again, v = log x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{1}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dv}}{/tex} = 2x log x log x
solution: Let u = x log x and v = log x Now, log u = (log x) 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\text u} \frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = 2 log {tex}\text x \cdot \frac{1}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{2 \log \text x}{\text x} \cdot \text x^{\log \text x}{/tex} Again, v = log x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{1}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dv}}{/tex} = 2x log x log x
solution_html:
Let u = xlog x and v = log x
Now, log u = (log x)2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\text u} \frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = 2 log {tex}\text x \cdot \frac{1}{\text x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{2 \log \text x}{\text x} \cdot \text x^{\log \text x}{/tex}
Again, v = log x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{1}{\text x}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dv}}{/tex} = 2xlog x log x

question_html: Find the value of {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} at {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac \pi 3{/tex}, if x = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} - cos {tex}2\theta{/tex}, y = sin {tex}\theta{/tex} - sin {tex}2\theta{/tex}.
question_text: Find the value of {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} at {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac \pi 3{/tex} , if x = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} - cos {tex}2\theta{/tex} , y = sin {tex}\theta{/tex} - sin {tex}2\theta{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text d \theta}{/tex} = -sin {tex}\theta{/tex} + 2 sin {tex}2\theta{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text d \theta}{/tex} = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} - 2 cos {tex}2\theta{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\cos \theta-2 \cos 2 \theta}{-\sin \theta+2 \sin 2 \theta}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\left.\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}\right|_{\theta=\frac{\pi}{3}}=\sqrt{3}{/tex}
solution: {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text d \theta}{/tex} = -sin {tex}\theta{/tex} + 2 sin {tex}2\theta{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text d \theta}{/tex} = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} - 2 cos {tex}2\theta{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\cos \theta-2 \cos 2 \theta}{-\sin \theta+2 \sin 2 \theta}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\left.\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}\right|_{\theta=\frac{\pi}{3}}=\sqrt{3}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text d \theta}{/tex} = -sin {tex}\theta{/tex} + 2 sin {tex}2\theta{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text d \theta}{/tex} = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} - 2 cos {tex}2\theta{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\cos \theta-2 \cos 2 \theta}{-\sin \theta+2 \sin 2 \theta}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\left.\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}\right|_{\theta=\frac{\pi}{3}}=\sqrt{3}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If x = a cos{tex}\theta{/tex}, y = bsin {tex}\theta{/tex}, then find {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}^{2} \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{d} x^{2}}{/tex}
question_text: If x = a cos {tex}\theta{/tex} , y = bsin {tex}\theta{/tex} , then find {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}^{2} \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{d} x^{2}}{/tex}
explanation: {tex} x=a \cos \theta, y=b \sin \theta {/tex} {tex} \frac{d x}{d \theta}=-a \sin \theta, \frac{d y}{d \theta}=b \cos \theta {/tex} {tex} \therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\frac{d y}{d \theta}}{\frac{d x}{d \theta}}=\frac{b \cos \theta}{-a \sin \theta}=-\frac{b}{a} \cot \theta{/tex} {tex} \therefore \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{-b}{a} \cot \theta\right){/tex} {tex} =\frac{-b}{a} \times-\operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta \times \frac{d \theta}{d x}{/tex} {tex} =\frac{b}{a} \times \operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta \times \frac{1}{-a \sin \theta}{/tex} {tex} \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=\frac{-b}{a^3} \times \operatorname{cosec}^3 \theta{/tex}
solution: {tex} x=a \cos \theta, y=b \sin \theta {/tex} {tex} \frac{d x}{d \theta}=-a \sin \theta, \frac{d y}{d \theta}=b \cos \theta {/tex} {tex} \therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\frac{d y}{d \theta}}{\frac{d x}{d \theta}}=\frac{b \cos \theta}{-a \sin \theta}=-\frac{b}{a} \cot \theta{/tex} {tex} \therefore \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{-b}{a} \cot \theta\right){/tex} {tex} =\frac{-b}{a} \times-\operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta \times \frac{d \theta}{d x}{/tex} {tex} =\frac{b}{a} \times \operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta \times \frac{1}{-a \sin \theta}{/tex} {tex} \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=\frac{-b}{a^3} \times \operatorname{cosec}^3 \theta{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex} x=a \cos \theta, y=b \sin \theta {/tex}
{tex} \frac{d x}{d \theta}=-a \sin \theta, \frac{d y}{d \theta}=b \cos \theta {/tex}
{tex} \therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\frac{d y}{d \theta}}{\frac{d x}{d \theta}}=\frac{b \cos \theta}{-a \sin \theta}=-\frac{b}{a} \cot \theta{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{-b}{a} \cot \theta\right){/tex}
{tex} =\frac{-b}{a} \times-\operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta \times \frac{d \theta}{d x}{/tex}
{tex} =\frac{b}{a} \times \operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta \times \frac{1}{-a \sin \theta}{/tex}
{tex} \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=\frac{-b}{a^3} \times \operatorname{cosec}^3 \theta{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If y = {tex}e^{x^{2} \cos x}{/tex} + (cos x)x, then find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
question_text: If y = {tex}e^{x^{2} \cos x}{/tex} + (cos x) x , then find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
explanation: Let {tex}u=(\cos x)^x \Rightarrow y=e^{x^2 \cdot \cos x}+u{/tex} {tex} \therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x^2 \cdot \cos x}\left(2 x \cdot \cos x-x^2 \cdot \sin x\right)+\frac{d u}{d x} {/tex} {tex} \log u=\log (\cos x)^x \Rightarrow \log u=x \cdot \log (\cos x) {/tex} Differentiate w.r.t. "x" {tex} \frac{1}{u} \frac{d u}{d x}=\log (\cos x)-x \tan x {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=(\cos x)^x\{\log (\cos x)-x \tan x\}{/tex} Therefore, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x^2 \cdot \cos x}\left(2 x \cdot \cos x-x^2 \cdot \sin x\right)+(\cos x)^x\{\log (\cos x)-x \tan x\}{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}u=(\cos x)^x \Rightarrow y=e^{x^2 \cdot \cos x}+u{/tex} {tex} \therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x^2 \cdot \cos x}\left(2 x \cdot \cos x-x^2 \cdot \sin x\right)+\frac{d u}{d x} {/tex} {tex} \log u=\log (\cos x)^x \Rightarrow \log u=x \cdot \log (\cos x) {/tex} Differentiate w.r.t. "x" {tex} \frac{1}{u} \frac{d u}{d x}=\log (\cos x)-x \tan x {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=(\cos x)^x\{\log (\cos x)-x \tan x\}{/tex} Therefore, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x^2 \cdot \cos x}\left(2 x \cdot \cos x-x^2 \cdot \sin x\right)+(\cos x)^x\{\log (\cos x)-x \tan x\}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}u=(\cos x)^x \Rightarrow y=e^{x^2 \cdot \cos x}+u{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x^2 \cdot \cos x}\left(2 x \cdot \cos x-x^2 \cdot \sin x\right)+\frac{d u}{d x} {/tex}
{tex} \log u=\log (\cos x)^x \Rightarrow \log u=x \cdot \log (\cos x) {/tex}
Differentiate w.r.t. "x"
{tex} \frac{1}{u} \frac{d u}{d x}=\log (\cos x)-x \tan x {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=(\cos x)^x\{\log (\cos x)-x \tan x\}{/tex}
Therefore, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x^2 \cdot \cos x}\left(2 x \cdot \cos x-x^2 \cdot \sin x\right)+(\cos x)^x\{\log (\cos x)-x \tan x\}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Solve the differential equation:
{tex}x \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{x}\right) \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}+x-\mathrm{y} \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{x}\right)=0{/tex}
Given that x = 1 when y = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
question_text: Solve the differential equation: {tex}x \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{x}\right) \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}+x-\mathrm{y} \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{x}\right)=0{/tex} Given that x = 1 when y = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
explanation: {tex} x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}+x-y \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=0 .{/tex} {tex} x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}=y \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-x {/tex} {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-x}{x\left(\sin \frac{y}{x}\right)} {/tex} Now {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{y}{x}-\operatorname{cosec}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} nut {tex}x=\lambda x, y=\lambda y{/tex} {tex} f(\lambda x, \lambda y) =\frac{\lambda y}{\lambda x}-\operatorname{cosec}\left(\frac{\lambda y}{\lambda x}\right) {/tex} {tex} =\lambda^0\left[\frac{y}{x}-\operatorname{cosec}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)\right] {/tex} {tex} =\lambda^0 f(x, y) {/tex} So it is homogeneous. Now put, {tex}y=v x{/tex} ins eq n (1) get, and {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} . we get. {tex} v+x \frac{d v}{d x} =\frac{v x \sin \left(\frac{v x}{x}\right)-x}{x \sin \frac{v x}{x}} {/tex} {tex} +v+x \frac{d v}{d x} =\frac{x(v \sin v-1)}{x \sin v} {/tex} {tex} x+x \frac{d v}{d x} =v-\frac{1}{\sin v} {/tex} {tex} x \frac{d v}{d x} =-\frac{1}{\sin v} {/tex} {tex} x \frac{d v}{d x}=-\frac{1}{\sin v} {/tex} {tex} -\int \sin v d v=\int \frac{d x}{x} {/tex} {tex} \cos v=\log |x|+C {/tex} {tex} \text { or } \cos \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=\log |x|+C {/tex} given that, {tex} x=1 \text { \} y=\pi / 2 {/tex} {tex} \cos \left(\frac{\pi / 2}{1}\right)=\log 1+c {/tex} {tex} 0=0+c {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow c=0 {/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Particular Sol {tex}{ }^{\circ}{/tex} is {tex} \cos \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=\log |x|{/tex}
solution: {tex} x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}+x-y \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=0 .{/tex} {tex} x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}=y \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-x {/tex} {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-x}{x\left(\sin \frac{y}{x}\right)} {/tex} Now {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{y}{x}-\operatorname{cosec}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} nut {tex}x=\lambda x, y=\lambda y{/tex} {tex} f(\lambda x, \lambda y) =\frac{\lambda y}{\lambda x}-\operatorname{cosec}\left(\frac{\lambda y}{\lambda x}\right) {/tex} {tex} =\lambda^0\left[\frac{y}{x}-\operatorname{cosec}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)\right] {/tex} {tex} =\lambda^0 f(x, y) {/tex} So it is homogeneous. Now put, {tex}y=v x{/tex} ins eq n (1) get, and {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} . we get. {tex} v+x \frac{d v}{d x} =\frac{v x \sin \left(\frac{v x}{x}\right)-x}{x \sin \frac{v x}{x}} {/tex} {tex} +v+x \frac{d v}{d x} =\frac{x(v \sin v-1)}{x \sin v} {/tex} {tex} x+x \frac{d v}{d x} =v-\frac{1}{\sin v} {/tex} {tex} x \frac{d v}{d x} =-\frac{1}{\sin v} {/tex} {tex} x \frac{d v}{d x}=-\frac{1}{\sin v} {/tex} {tex} -\int \sin v d v=\int \frac{d x}{x} {/tex} {tex} \cos v=\log |x|+C {/tex} {tex} \text { or } \cos \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=\log |x|+C {/tex} given that, {tex} x=1 \text { \} y=\pi / 2 {/tex} {tex} \cos \left(\frac{\pi / 2}{1}\right)=\log 1+c {/tex} {tex} 0=0+c {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow c=0 {/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Particular Sol {tex}{ }^{\circ}{/tex} is {tex} \cos \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=\log |x|{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex} x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}+x-y \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=0 .{/tex}


{tex} x \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}=y \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-x {/tex}
{tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-x}{x\left(\sin \frac{y}{x}\right)} {/tex}


Now {tex}f(x, y)=\frac{y}{x}-\operatorname{cosec}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex}
nut {tex}x=\lambda x, y=\lambda y{/tex}

{tex} f(\lambda x, \lambda y) =\frac{\lambda y}{\lambda x}-\operatorname{cosec}\left(\frac{\lambda y}{\lambda x}\right) {/tex}
{tex} =\lambda^0\left[\frac{y}{x}-\operatorname{cosec}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)\right] {/tex}
{tex} =\lambda^0 f(x, y) {/tex}


So it is homogeneous.
Now put, {tex}y=v x{/tex} ins eq n (1) get, and {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex}. we get.

{tex} v+x \frac{d v}{d x} =\frac{v x \sin \left(\frac{v x}{x}\right)-x}{x \sin \frac{v x}{x}} {/tex}
{tex} +v+x \frac{d v}{d x} =\frac{x(v \sin v-1)}{x \sin v} {/tex}
{tex} x+x \frac{d v}{d x} =v-\frac{1}{\sin v} {/tex}
{tex} x \frac{d v}{d x} =-\frac{1}{\sin v} {/tex}

 

{tex} x \frac{d v}{d x}=-\frac{1}{\sin v} {/tex}
{tex} -\int \sin v d v=\int \frac{d x}{x} {/tex}
{tex} \cos v=\log |x|+C {/tex}
{tex} \text { or } \cos \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=\log |x|+C {/tex}

given that,

{tex} x=1 \text { \} y=\pi / 2 {/tex}
{tex} \cos \left(\frac{\pi / 2}{1}\right)=\log 1+c {/tex}
{tex} 0=0+c {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow c=0 {/tex}

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Particular Sol {tex}{ }^{\circ}{/tex} is

{tex} \cos \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=\log |x|{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find f'(x) if f(x) = (tan x)tan x.
question_text: Find f'(x) if f(x) = (tan x) tan x .
explanation: Let {tex}y=f(x){/tex} -i.e. {tex}y=(\tan x)^{\tan x}{/tex} taking. {tex}\log{/tex} both sides we get, {tex} \log y=\tan x \cdot \log \tan x{/tex} diff. w. rt. {tex}x{/tex} we get; {tex} \frac{-}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}=\tan x \frac{d}{d x} \log \tan x+\log \tan x \cdot \frac{d}{d x} \tan x {/tex} {tex} \frac{1}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}=\tan x \cdot \frac{1}{\tan x} \frac{d}{d x} \tan x+\log \tan x \cdot \sec ^2 x {/tex} {tex} =\sec ^2 x+\log \tan x \cdot \sec ^2 x {/tex} {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=y\left[\sec ^2 x(1+\log \tan x)\right]=(\tan x)^{\tan x} \cdot \sec ^2 x(1+\log \tan x) {/tex} {tex} \left.f^{\prime}(x)=(\tan x)^{\tan x} \sec ^2 x(1+\log \tan x)\right] {/tex}
solution: Let {tex}y=f(x){/tex} -i.e. {tex}y=(\tan x)^{\tan x}{/tex} taking. {tex}\log{/tex} both sides we get, {tex} \log y=\tan x \cdot \log \tan x{/tex} diff. w. rt. {tex}x{/tex} we get; {tex} \frac{-}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}=\tan x \frac{d}{d x} \log \tan x+\log \tan x \cdot \frac{d}{d x} \tan x {/tex} {tex} \frac{1}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}=\tan x \cdot \frac{1}{\tan x} \frac{d}{d x} \tan x+\log \tan x \cdot \sec ^2 x {/tex} {tex} =\sec ^2 x+\log \tan x \cdot \sec ^2 x {/tex} {tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=y\left[\sec ^2 x(1+\log \tan x)\right]=(\tan x)^{\tan x} \cdot \sec ^2 x(1+\log \tan x) {/tex} {tex} \left.f^{\prime}(x)=(\tan x)^{\tan x} \sec ^2 x(1+\log \tan x)\right] {/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}y=f(x){/tex}
-i.e. {tex}y=(\tan x)^{\tan x}{/tex}
taking. {tex}\log{/tex} both sides we get,

{tex} \log y=\tan x \cdot \log \tan x{/tex}

diff. w. rt. {tex}x{/tex} we get;

{tex} \frac{-}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}=\tan x \frac{d}{d x} \log \tan x+\log \tan x \cdot \frac{d}{d x} \tan x {/tex}
{tex} \frac{1}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}=\tan x \cdot \frac{1}{\tan x} \frac{d}{d x} \tan x+\log \tan x \cdot \sec ^2 x {/tex}
{tex} =\sec ^2 x+\log \tan x \cdot \sec ^2 x {/tex}
{tex} \frac{d y}{d x}=y\left[\sec ^2 x(1+\log \tan x)\right]=(\tan x)^{\tan x} \cdot \sec ^2 x(1+\log \tan x) {/tex}
{tex} \left.f^{\prime}(x)=(\tan x)^{\tan x} \sec ^2 x(1+\log \tan x)\right] {/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If y = (x)cos x + (cos x)sin x, find {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex}.
question_text: If y = (x) cos x + (cos x) sin x , find {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} .
explanation: Let y = u + v, where u = (x) cos x and v = (cos x) sin x Now, u = (x) cos x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log u = (cos x)(log x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{u} \cdot \frac{d u}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}(\cos x) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\log x){/tex} + {tex}(\log x) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\cos x){/tex} [on differentiating w.r.t. x] = {tex}(\cos x) \cdot \frac{1}{x}{/tex} + (log x)(-sin x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d u}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}u \cdot\left\{\frac{\cos x}{x}-\right.{/tex} (log x)(sin x)} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d u}{d x}{/tex} = (x) cos x { {tex}\frac{\cos x}{x}{/tex} - (log x)(sin x)} ......(i) And, v = (cos x) sin x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log v = (sin x) log (cos x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{v} \cdot \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}(\sin x) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\{\log (\cos x)\}{/tex} + {tex}\log (\cos x) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\sin x){/tex} [on differentiating w.r.t. x] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}v \cdot\left\{(\sin x) \cdot \frac{(-\sin x)}{\cos x}\right.{/tex} + log (cos x) {tex}\cdot{/tex} cos x} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = (cos x) sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} {-sin x tan x + cos x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (cos x)} ....(ii) {tex}\therefore{/tex} y = (u = v) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = (x) cos x { {tex}\frac{\cos x}{x}{/tex} - (log x)(sin x)} + (cos x) sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} {-sin x tan x + cos x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (cos x)}
solution: Let y = u + v, where u = (x) cos x and v = (cos x) sin x Now, u = (x) cos x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log u = (cos x)(log x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{u} \cdot \frac{d u}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}(\cos x) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\log x){/tex} + {tex}(\log x) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\cos x){/tex} [on differentiating w.r.t. x] = {tex}(\cos x) \cdot \frac{1}{x}{/tex} + (log x)(-sin x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d u}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}u \cdot\left\{\frac{\cos x}{x}-\right.{/tex} (log x)(sin x)} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d u}{d x}{/tex} = (x) cos x { {tex}\frac{\cos x}{x}{/tex} - (log x)(sin x)} ......(i) And, v = (cos x) sin x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log v = (sin x) log (cos x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{v} \cdot \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}(\sin x) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\{\log (\cos x)\}{/tex} + {tex}\log (\cos x) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\sin x){/tex} [on differentiating w.r.t. x] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}v \cdot\left\{(\sin x) \cdot \frac{(-\sin x)}{\cos x}\right.{/tex} + log (cos x) {tex}\cdot{/tex} cos x} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = (cos x) sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} {-sin x tan x + cos x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (cos x)} ....(ii) {tex}\therefore{/tex} y = (u = v) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = (x) cos x { {tex}\frac{\cos x}{x}{/tex} - (log x)(sin x)} + (cos x) sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} {-sin x tan x + cos x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (cos x)}
solution_html:
Let y = u + v, where u = (x)cos x and v = (cos x)sin x
Now, u = (x)cos x 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log u = (cos x)(log x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{u} \cdot \frac{d u}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}(\cos x) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\log x){/tex} + {tex}(\log x) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\cos x){/tex} [on differentiating w.r.t. x]
= {tex}(\cos x) \cdot \frac{1}{x}{/tex} + (log x)(-sin x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d u}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}u \cdot\left\{\frac{\cos x}{x}-\right.{/tex} (log x)(sin x)}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d u}{d x}{/tex} = (x)cos x {{tex}\frac{\cos x}{x}{/tex} - (log x)(sin x)} ......(i)
And, v = (cos x)sin x 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log v = (sin x) log (cos x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{v} \cdot \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}(\sin x) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\{\log (\cos x)\}{/tex} + {tex}\log (\cos x) \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\sin x){/tex} [on differentiating w.r.t. x]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}v \cdot\left\{(\sin x) \cdot \frac{(-\sin x)}{\cos x}\right.{/tex} + log (cos x) {tex}\cdot{/tex} cos x}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = (cos x)sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} {-sin x tan x + cos x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (cos x)}  ....(ii)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} y = (u = v)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = (x)cos x {{tex}\frac{\cos x}{x}{/tex} - (log x)(sin x)} + (cos x)sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} {-sin x tan x + cos x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (cos x)}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If (cos x)y = (sin y)x, find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
question_text: If (cos x) y = (sin y) x , find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
explanation: Given, (cos x) y = (sin y) x On taking log on both sides, we get y log(cosx) = x log(siny) ...(i) On differentiating equation (i) w.r.t. x, {tex}\Rightarrow y \times \frac{d}{d x}(\log \cos x)+\log \cos x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}\log \sin y \frac{d x}{d x}+x \frac{d}{d x}(\log \sin y){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \times \frac{1}{\cos x}(-\sin x)+\log \cos x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}\log \sin y+x \cdot \frac{\cos y}{\sin y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow-y \tan x+\frac{d y}{d x} \log \cos x=\log \sin y+x \cot y \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}(\log \cos x-x \cot y)=\log \sin y+y \tan x{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\log \sin y+y \tan x}{\log \cos x-x \cot y}{/tex}
solution: Given, (cos x) y = (sin y) x On taking log on both sides, we get y log(cosx) = x log(siny) ...(i) On differentiating equation (i) w.r.t. x, {tex}\Rightarrow y \times \frac{d}{d x}(\log \cos x)+\log \cos x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}\log \sin y \frac{d x}{d x}+x \frac{d}{d x}(\log \sin y){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \times \frac{1}{\cos x}(-\sin x)+\log \cos x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}\log \sin y+x \cdot \frac{\cos y}{\sin y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow-y \tan x+\frac{d y}{d x} \log \cos x=\log \sin y+x \cot y \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}(\log \cos x-x \cot y)=\log \sin y+y \tan x{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\log \sin y+y \tan x}{\log \cos x-x \cot y}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given, (cos x)y = (sin y)x
On taking log on both sides, we get
y log(cosx) = x log(siny) ...(i)
On differentiating equation (i) w.r.t. x,
{tex}\Rightarrow y \times \frac{d}{d x}(\log \cos x)+\log \cos x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
= {tex}\log \sin y \frac{d x}{d x}+x \frac{d}{d x}(\log \sin y){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow y \times \frac{1}{\cos x}(-\sin x)+\log \cos x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
= {tex}\log \sin y+x \cdot \frac{\cos y}{\sin y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow-y \tan x+\frac{d y}{d x} \log \cos x=\log \sin y+x \cot y \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}(\log \cos x-x \cot y)=\log \sin y+y \tan x{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\log \sin y+y \tan x}{\log \cos x-x \cot y}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If y = (cot-1 x)2, show that {tex}\left(x^2+1\right)^2 \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}+2 x\left(x^2+1\right) \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = 2.
question_text: If y = (cot -1 x) 2 , show that {tex}\left(x^2+1\right)^2 \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}+2 x\left(x^2+1\right) \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = 2.
explanation: y = (cot -1 x) 2 Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}2 \cot ^{-1} x\left(\frac{-1}{1+x^{2}}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{-2 \cot ^{-1} x}{1+x^{2}}{/tex} Again differentiate the both sides w.r.t. x, {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}-2\left[\frac{\left(1+x^{2}\right)-\frac{1}{1+x^{2}}-\cot ^{-1} x(2 x)}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right]{/tex} {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}-2\left[\frac{-1-2 x \cot ^{-1} x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2+4 x \cdot \cot ^{-1} x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex} LHS = {tex}\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left\{\frac{2+4 x \cot ^{-1} x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right\}{/tex} + {tex}2 x\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left\{\frac{-2 \cot ^{-1} x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)}\right\}{/tex} = 2 + 4x cot -1 x - 4xcot -1 x = 2 = RHS
solution: y = (cot -1 x) 2 Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}2 \cot ^{-1} x\left(\frac{-1}{1+x^{2}}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{-2 \cot ^{-1} x}{1+x^{2}}{/tex} Again differentiate the both sides w.r.t. x, {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}-2\left[\frac{\left(1+x^{2}\right)-\frac{1}{1+x^{2}}-\cot ^{-1} x(2 x)}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right]{/tex} {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}-2\left[\frac{-1-2 x \cot ^{-1} x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2+4 x \cdot \cot ^{-1} x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex} LHS = {tex}\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left\{\frac{2+4 x \cot ^{-1} x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right\}{/tex} + {tex}2 x\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left\{\frac{-2 \cot ^{-1} x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)}\right\}{/tex} = 2 + 4x cot -1 x - 4xcot -1 x = 2 = RHS
solution_html:
y = (cot-1 x)2 
Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}2 \cot ^{-1} x\left(\frac{-1}{1+x^{2}}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{-2 \cot ^{-1} x}{1+x^{2}}{/tex} 
Again differentiate the both sides w.r.t. x,
{tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}-2\left[\frac{\left(1+x^{2}\right)-\frac{1}{1+x^{2}}-\cot ^{-1} x(2 x)}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}-2\left[\frac{-1-2 x \cot ^{-1} x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2+4 x \cdot \cot ^{-1} x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex} 
LHS = {tex}\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left\{\frac{2+4 x \cot ^{-1} x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}}\right\}{/tex} + {tex}2 x\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left\{\frac{-2 \cot ^{-1} x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)}\right\}{/tex} 
= 2 + 4x cot-1 x - 4xcot-1 x = 2 = RHS

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If sin y = x sin (a + y), prove that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}{\sin a}{/tex}
question_text: If sin y = x sin (a + y), prove that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}{\sin a}{/tex}
explanation: {tex}\sin y=x \cdot \sin (a+y){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{\sin y}{\sin (a+y)}{/tex} ...(i) Differentiating equation (i) w.r.t. y, we get {tex}\frac{d x}{d y}=\frac{\sin (a+y) \cos y-\sin y \cos (a+y)}{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}{/tex} {tex}\frac{d x}{d y}=\frac{\sin (a+y-y)}{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}=\frac{\sin a}{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}{\sin a}{/tex}
solution: {tex}\sin y=x \cdot \sin (a+y){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{\sin y}{\sin (a+y)}{/tex} ...(i) Differentiating equation (i) w.r.t. y, we get {tex}\frac{d x}{d y}=\frac{\sin (a+y) \cos y-\sin y \cos (a+y)}{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}{/tex} {tex}\frac{d x}{d y}=\frac{\sin (a+y-y)}{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}=\frac{\sin a}{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}{\sin a}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\sin y=x \cdot \sin (a+y){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{\sin y}{\sin (a+y)}{/tex} ...(i)
Differentiating equation (i) w.r.t. y, we get
{tex}\frac{d x}{d y}=\frac{\sin (a+y) \cos y-\sin y \cos (a+y)}{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{d x}{d y}=\frac{\sin (a+y-y)}{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}=\frac{\sin a}{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sin ^{2}(a+y)}{\sin a}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}x\sqrt{1+y}+y \sqrt{1+x}=0{/tex} and x {tex}\ne{/tex} y, prove that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{1}{(x+1)^{2}}{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}x\sqrt{1+y}+y \sqrt{1+x}=0{/tex} and x {tex}\ne{/tex} y, prove that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=-\frac{1}{(x+1)^{2}}{/tex} .
explanation: According to the question,we have to prove that {tex}\frac { d y } { d x } = - \frac { 1 } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} if {tex}x \sqrt { 1 + y } + y \sqrt { 1 + x } = 0{/tex} where x {tex}\ne{/tex} y. we shall first write y in terms of x explicitly i.e y=f(x) Clearly, {tex}x \sqrt { 1 + y } = - y \sqrt { 1 + x }{/tex} Squaring both sides,we get, x 2 (1 + y) = y 2 (1 + x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 + x 2 y = y 2 (1 + x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 - y 2 = y 2 x - x 2 y {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - y)(x + y) = -xy(x - y) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - y)(x + y) + xy(x - y) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - y)(x + y + xy) = 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} Either, x - y = 0 or x + y + xy = 0 Now, x - y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = y But, it is given that x {tex}\ne{/tex} y So, it is a contradiction {tex}{/tex} Therefore, x - y = 0 is rejected. Now, consider y + xy + x = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y(1 + x) = -x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = {tex}\frac { - x } { 1 + x }{/tex} ..........(i) Therefore,on differentiating both sides w.r.t x, we get, {tex}\frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { ( 1 + x ) \times \frac { d } { d x } ( - x ) - ( - x ) \times \frac { d } { d x } ( 1 + x ) } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} [By using quotient rule of derivative] {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { ( 1 + x ) ( - 1 ) + x ( 1 ) } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { - 1 - x + x } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { - 1 } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
solution: According to the question,we have to prove that {tex}\frac { d y } { d x } = - \frac { 1 } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} if {tex}x \sqrt { 1 + y } + y \sqrt { 1 + x } = 0{/tex} where x {tex}\ne{/tex} y. we shall first write y in terms of x explicitly i.e y=f(x) Clearly, {tex}x \sqrt { 1 + y } = - y \sqrt { 1 + x }{/tex} Squaring both sides,we get, x 2 (1 + y) = y 2 (1 + x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 + x 2 y = y 2 (1 + x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 - y 2 = y 2 x - x 2 y {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - y)(x + y) = -xy(x - y) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - y)(x + y) + xy(x - y) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - y)(x + y + xy) = 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} Either, x - y = 0 or x + y + xy = 0 Now, x - y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = y But, it is given that x {tex}\ne{/tex} y So, it is a contradiction {tex}{/tex} Therefore, x - y = 0 is rejected. Now, consider y + xy + x = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y(1 + x) = -x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = {tex}\frac { - x } { 1 + x }{/tex} ..........(i) Therefore,on differentiating both sides w.r.t x, we get, {tex}\frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { ( 1 + x ) \times \frac { d } { d x } ( - x ) - ( - x ) \times \frac { d } { d x } ( 1 + x ) } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} [By using quotient rule of derivative] {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { ( 1 + x ) ( - 1 ) + x ( 1 ) } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { - 1 - x + x } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { - 1 } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question,we have to prove that  {tex}\frac { d y } { d x } = - \frac { 1 } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} if {tex}x \sqrt { 1 + y } + y \sqrt { 1 + x } = 0{/tex}
where x{tex}\ne{/tex}y.

we shall first write y in terms of x explicitly i.e  y=f(x)
Clearly, {tex}x \sqrt { 1 + y } = - y \sqrt { 1 + x }{/tex}

Squaring both sides,we get,
x2(1 + y) = y2 (1 + x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}x2 + x2y = y2(1 + x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}x2 - y2 = y2x - x2y
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}(x - y)(x + y) = -xy(x - y)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}(x - y)(x + y) + xy(x - y) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}(x - y)(x + y + xy) = 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex}Either, x - y  = 0 or x + y + xy = 0
Now, x - y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = y
But, it is given that x {tex}\ne{/tex} y
So, it is a contradiction 
{tex}{/tex}Therefore, x - y = 0 is rejected.
Now, consider y + xy + x = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y(1 + x) = -x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}y = {tex}\frac { - x } { 1 + x }{/tex}..........(i)
Therefore,on differentiating both sides w.r.t x, we get,
{tex}\frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { ( 1 + x ) \times \frac { d } { d x } ( - x ) - ( - x ) \times \frac { d } { d x } ( 1 + x ) } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}[By using quotient rule of derivative]
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { ( 1 + x ) ( - 1 ) + x ( 1 ) } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { - 1 - x + x } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { - 1 } { ( 1 + x ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If x = cos t + log tan {tex}(\frac {t} {2}){/tex}, y = sin t, then find the values of {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{dt}^2}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{dx}^2}{/tex} at t = {tex}\frac {\pi} {4}{/tex}.
question_text: If x = cos t + log tan {tex}(\frac {t} {2}){/tex} , y = sin t, then find the values of {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{dt}^2}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{dx}^2}{/tex} at t = {tex}\frac {\pi} {4}{/tex} .
explanation: Given that, x = cos t + log tan {tex}\frac{t}{2}{/tex} , y = sin t Differentiating with respect to t, we have, {tex}\frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\tan \frac{t}{2}} \times \sec ^{2} \frac{t}{2} \times \frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\frac{\sin \frac{t}{2}}{\cos \frac{t}{2}}} \times \frac{1}{\cos ^{2} \frac{t}{2}} \times \frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{2 \sin \frac{t}{2} \cos \frac{t}{2}}{/tex} {tex}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\sin t}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1-\sin ^{2} t}{\sin t}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\cos ^{2} t}{\sin t}{/tex} = cos t {tex}\times{/tex} cot t Now find the value of {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}{/tex} : {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}{/tex} = cos t Thus, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y}{d t} \times \frac{d t}{d x}=\cos t \times \frac{1}{\cos t \times \cot t}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\tan t{/tex} Since {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}{/tex} = cos t, we have {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d t^{2}}=-\sin t{/tex} At {tex}t=\frac{\pi}{4},\left(\frac{d^{2} y}{d t^{2}}\right)_{t=\frac{\pi}{4}}=-\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{\frac{d}{d t}\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)}{\frac{d x}{d t}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\frac{d}{d t}(\tan t)}{\cos t \times \cot t}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\sec ^{2} t}{\cos t \times \cot t}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\sec ^{2} t}{\cos t \times \frac{\cos t}{\sin t}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\sec ^{2} t}{\cos ^{2} t} \times \sin t{/tex} = sec 4 t {tex}\times{/tex} sin t Thus, {tex}\left(\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}\right)_{t=\frac{\pi}{4}}=\sec ^{4}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \times \sin \frac{\pi}{4}=2 \sqrt2{/tex}
solution: Given that, x = cos t + log tan {tex}\frac{t}{2}{/tex} , y = sin t Differentiating with respect to t, we have, {tex}\frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\tan \frac{t}{2}} \times \sec ^{2} \frac{t}{2} \times \frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\frac{\sin \frac{t}{2}}{\cos \frac{t}{2}}} \times \frac{1}{\cos ^{2} \frac{t}{2}} \times \frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{2 \sin \frac{t}{2} \cos \frac{t}{2}}{/tex} {tex}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\sin t}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1-\sin ^{2} t}{\sin t}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\cos ^{2} t}{\sin t}{/tex} = cos t {tex}\times{/tex} cot t Now find the value of {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}{/tex} : {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}{/tex} = cos t Thus, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y}{d t} \times \frac{d t}{d x}=\cos t \times \frac{1}{\cos t \times \cot t}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\tan t{/tex} Since {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}{/tex} = cos t, we have {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d t^{2}}=-\sin t{/tex} At {tex}t=\frac{\pi}{4},\left(\frac{d^{2} y}{d t^{2}}\right)_{t=\frac{\pi}{4}}=-\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{\frac{d}{d t}\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)}{\frac{d x}{d t}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\frac{d}{d t}(\tan t)}{\cos t \times \cot t}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\sec ^{2} t}{\cos t \times \cot t}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\sec ^{2} t}{\cos t \times \frac{\cos t}{\sin t}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\sec ^{2} t}{\cos ^{2} t} \times \sin t{/tex} = sec 4 t {tex}\times{/tex} sin t Thus, {tex}\left(\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}\right)_{t=\frac{\pi}{4}}=\sec ^{4}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \times \sin \frac{\pi}{4}=2 \sqrt2{/tex}
solution_html:
Given that, x = cos t + log tan {tex}\frac{t}{2}{/tex}, y = sin t
Differentiating with respect to t, we have,
{tex}\frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\tan \frac{t}{2}} \times \sec ^{2} \frac{t}{2} \times \frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\frac{\sin \frac{t}{2}}{\cos \frac{t}{2}}} \times \frac{1}{\cos ^{2} \frac{t}{2}} \times \frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{2 \sin \frac{t}{2} \cos \frac{t}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\sin t}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1-\sin ^{2} t}{\sin t}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\cos ^{2} t}{\sin t}{/tex}
= cos t {tex}\times{/tex} cot t
Now find the value of {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}{/tex}:
{tex}\frac{d y}{d t}{/tex} = cos t
Thus, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y}{d t} \times \frac{d t}{d x}=\cos t \times \frac{1}{\cos t \times \cot t}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\tan t{/tex}
Since {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}{/tex} = cos t, we have {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d t^{2}}=-\sin t{/tex}
At {tex}t=\frac{\pi}{4},\left(\frac{d^{2} y}{d t^{2}}\right)_{t=\frac{\pi}{4}}=-\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{\frac{d}{d t}\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)}{\frac{d x}{d t}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\frac{d}{d t}(\tan t)}{\cos t \times \cot t}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\sec ^{2} t}{\cos t \times \cot t}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\sec ^{2} t}{\cos t \times \frac{\cos t}{\sin t}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\sec ^{2} t}{\cos ^{2} t} \times \sin t{/tex}
= sec4 t {tex}\times{/tex} sin t
Thus, {tex}\left(\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}\right)_{t=\frac{\pi}{4}}=\sec ^{4}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \times \sin \frac{\pi}{4}=2 \sqrt2{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If x = aet (sin t + cos t) and y = aet (sin t – cos t), prove that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex}.
question_text: If x = ae t (sin t + cos t) and y = ae t (sin t – cos t), prove that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex} .
explanation: x = ae t (sin t + cos t) and y = ae t (sin t - cos t) (Given) {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{dx}{dt}{/tex} = a[e t (cos t - sin t) + e t (sin t + cos t)] = -y + x and {tex}\frac{dy}{dt}{/tex} = a[e t (sin t - cos t) + e t (sin t + cos t)] = y + x Therefore {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y / d t}{d x / d t}{/tex} or {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex}
solution: x = ae t (sin t + cos t) and y = ae t (sin t - cos t) (Given) {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{dx}{dt}{/tex} = a[e t (cos t - sin t) + e t (sin t + cos t)] = -y + x and {tex}\frac{dy}{dt}{/tex} = a[e t (sin t - cos t) + e t (sin t + cos t)] = y + x Therefore {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y / d t}{d x / d t}{/tex} or {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex}
solution_html:
x = aet (sin t + cos t) and y = aet (sin t - cos t)   (Given)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{dx}{dt}{/tex} = a[et(cos t - sin t) + et(sin t + cos t)]
= -y + x
and {tex}\frac{dy}{dt}{/tex} = a[et (sin t - cos t) + et(sin t + cos t)]
= y + x
Therefore
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y / d t}{d x / d t}{/tex} 
or {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex}
