question_html: If x = sin t, y = sin pt, prove that {tex}\left(1-x^2\right) \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}-x \frac{d y}{d x}+p^2 y=0{/tex}.
question_text: If x = sin t, y = sin pt, prove that {tex}\left(1-x^2\right) \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}-x \frac{d y}{d x}+p^2 y=0{/tex} .
explanation: We have, x = sint and y = sin pt, {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \cos t{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \cos pt.p{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{p.\cos pt}}{{\cos t}}{/tex} ...(i) Again, differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{\cos t.\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {p.\cos pt} \right)\frac{{dt}}{{dx}} - p\cos pt.\frac{d}{{dt}}\cos t.\frac{{dt}}{{dx}}}}{{{{\cos }^2}t}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\left[ {\cos t.p.\left( { - \sin pt} \right).p - p\cos pt.\left( { - \sin t} \right)} \right]\frac{{dt}}{{dx}}}}{{{{\cos }^2}t}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\left[ { - {p^2}\sin pt.\cos t + p\ sint.cospt} \right].\frac{1}{{\cos t}}}}{{{{\cos }^2}t}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - {p^2}\sin pt.\cos t + p\cos pt.\sin t}}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}{/tex} ...(ii) Since, we have to prove {tex}\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} - x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + {p^2}y = 0{/tex} {tex}\therefore LHS = \left( {1 - {{\sin }^2}t} \right)\frac{{\left[- {{p^2}\ sinpt.\ cost + p\ cospt .\sin t} \right]}}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}{/tex} {tex}- \ sint .\frac{{p\cos pt}}{{\cos t}} + {p^2}\sin pt{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}\left[ \begin{gathered} \left( {1 - {{\sin }^2}t} \right)\left( { - {p^2}\sin pt.\cos t + p\cos pt.\sin t} \right) \hfill \\ - p\cos pt.\sin t.{\cos ^2}t + {p^2}\sin pt.{\cos ^3}t \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}\left[ \begin{gathered} - {p^2}\sin pt.{\cos ^3}t + p\cos pt.\sin t.{\cos ^2}t \hfill \\ - p\cos pt.\sin t.{\cos ^2}t + {p^2}\sin pt.{\cos ^3}t \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because 1 - {{\sin }^2}t = {{\cos }^2}t} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}.0{/tex} = 0 Hence proved.
solution: We have, x = sint and y = sin pt, {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \cos t{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \cos pt.p{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{p.\cos pt}}{{\cos t}}{/tex} ...(i) Again, differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{\cos t.\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {p.\cos pt} \right)\frac{{dt}}{{dx}} - p\cos pt.\frac{d}{{dt}}\cos t.\frac{{dt}}{{dx}}}}{{{{\cos }^2}t}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\left[ {\cos t.p.\left( { - \sin pt} \right).p - p\cos pt.\left( { - \sin t} \right)} \right]\frac{{dt}}{{dx}}}}{{{{\cos }^2}t}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\left[ { - {p^2}\sin pt.\cos t + p\ sint.cospt} \right].\frac{1}{{\cos t}}}}{{{{\cos }^2}t}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - {p^2}\sin pt.\cos t + p\cos pt.\sin t}}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}{/tex} ...(ii) Since, we have to prove {tex}\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} - x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + {p^2}y = 0{/tex} {tex}\therefore LHS = \left( {1 - {{\sin }^2}t} \right)\frac{{\left[- {{p^2}\ sinpt.\ cost + p\ cospt .\sin t} \right]}}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}{/tex} {tex}- \ sint .\frac{{p\cos pt}}{{\cos t}} + {p^2}\sin pt{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}\left[ \begin{gathered} \left( {1 - {{\sin }^2}t} \right)\left( { - {p^2}\sin pt.\cos t + p\cos pt.\sin t} \right) \hfill \\ - p\cos pt.\sin t.{\cos ^2}t + {p^2}\sin pt.{\cos ^3}t \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}\left[ \begin{gathered} - {p^2}\sin pt.{\cos ^3}t + p\cos pt.\sin t.{\cos ^2}t \hfill \\ - p\cos pt.\sin t.{\cos ^2}t + {p^2}\sin pt.{\cos ^3}t \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because 1 - {{\sin }^2}t = {{\cos }^2}t} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}.0{/tex} = 0 Hence proved.
solution_html:
We have, x = sint and y = sin pt,
{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \cos t{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \cos pt.p{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{p.\cos pt}}{{\cos t}}{/tex} ...(i)
Again, differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get
{tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{\cos t.\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {p.\cos pt} \right)\frac{{dt}}{{dx}} - p\cos pt.\frac{d}{{dt}}\cos t.\frac{{dt}}{{dx}}}}{{{{\cos }^2}t}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{\left[ {\cos t.p.\left( { - \sin pt} \right).p - p\cos pt.\left( { - \sin t} \right)} \right]\frac{{dt}}{{dx}}}}{{{{\cos }^2}t}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{\left[ { - {p^2}\sin pt.\cos t + p\ sint.cospt} \right].\frac{1}{{\cos t}}}}{{{{\cos }^2}t}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - {p^2}\sin pt.\cos t + p\cos pt.\sin t}}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}{/tex} ...(ii)
Since, we have to prove
{tex}\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} - x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + {p^2}y = 0{/tex}
{tex}\therefore LHS = \left( {1 - {{\sin }^2}t} \right)\frac{{\left[- {{p^2}\ sinpt.\ cost + p\ cospt .\sin t} \right]}}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}{/tex}
{tex}- \ sint .\frac{{p\cos pt}}{{\cos t}} + {p^2}\sin pt{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{1}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}\left[ \begin{gathered} \left( {1 - {{\sin }^2}t} \right)\left( { - {p^2}\sin pt.\cos t + p\cos pt.\sin t} \right) \hfill \\ - p\cos pt.\sin t.{\cos ^2}t + {p^2}\sin pt.{\cos ^3}t \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}\left[ \begin{gathered} - {p^2}\sin pt.{\cos ^3}t + p\cos pt.\sin t.{\cos ^2}t \hfill \\ - p\cos pt.\sin t.{\cos ^2}t + {p^2}\sin pt.{\cos ^3}t \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex}{tex}\left[ {\because 1 - {{\sin }^2}t = {{\cos }^2}t} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{1}{{{{\cos }^3}t}}.0{/tex}
= 0 Hence proved.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If ey(x + 1) = 1, show that {tex}\frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } = \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 }{/tex}
question_text: If e y (x + 1) = 1, show that {tex}\frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } = \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 }{/tex}
explanation: According to the question, {tex}e^y (x + 1) = 1{/tex} Taking log both sides, {tex}\Rightarrow log [e^y (x + 1) ]= log 1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow log e^y + log(x + 1) = log 1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}y + log(x + 1) = log1{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} log e y = y] differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, {tex}\Rightarrow\frac { d y } { d x } + \frac { 1 } { x + 1 } = 0{/tex} .........(i) Differentiating both sides w.r.t. 'x', {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \frac { 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } } = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \left( - \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 } = 0{/tex} [ From Equation(i)] {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 } = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } = \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 }{/tex}
solution: According to the question, {tex}e^y (x + 1) = 1{/tex} Taking log both sides, {tex}\Rightarrow log [e^y (x + 1) ]= log 1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow log e^y + log(x + 1) = log 1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}y + log(x + 1) = log1{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} log e y = y] differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, {tex}\Rightarrow\frac { d y } { d x } + \frac { 1 } { x + 1 } = 0{/tex} .........(i) Differentiating both sides w.r.t. 'x', {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \frac { 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } } = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \left( - \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 } = 0{/tex} [ From Equation(i)] {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 } = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } = \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 }{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question, {tex}e^y (x + 1) = 1{/tex}
Taking log both sides, 
{tex}\Rightarrow log [e^y (x + 1) ]= log 1{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow log e^y + log(x + 1) = log 1{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}y + log(x + 1) = log1{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} log ey = y]
 differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, 
{tex}\Rightarrow\frac { d y } { d x } + \frac { 1 } { x + 1 } = 0{/tex}.........(i)
Differentiating both sides w.r.t. 'x', 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \frac { 1 } { ( x + 1 ) ^ { 2 } } = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \left( - \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 } = 0{/tex} [ From Equation(i)]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } - \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 } = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } = \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 }{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If xy + yx = ab, then find {tex}\frac { dy } { d x }{/tex}.
question_text: If x y + y x = a b , then find {tex}\frac { dy } { d x }{/tex} .
explanation: We have, x y + y x = a b .........(i) Let x y = v and y x = u......(ii) Therefore,on putting these values in Eq. (i), we get, v + u = a b Therefore,on differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get, {tex}\frac { d v } { d x } + \frac { d u } { d x } = 0{/tex} ........(iii) Now consider, x y = v [ from Eq.(ii)] Therefore,on taking log both sides, we get, log x y = logv {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log x = log v Therefore,on differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get, {tex}y \cdot \frac { 1 } { x } + \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { 1 } { v } \frac { d v } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow v \left( \frac { y } { x } + \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } \right) = \frac { d v } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d v } { d x } = x ^ { y } \left( \frac { y } { x } + \log x \frac { d y } { d x } \right){/tex} .........(iv) [ From Eq.(ii)] Also, y x = u [From Eq(ii)] Therefore,on taking log both sides, we get, log y x = log u {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x log y = log u Therefore,on differentiating both sides w.r.t. 'x', we get, {tex}x \cdot \frac { 1 } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + 1 \cdot \log y = \frac { 1 } { u } \frac { d u } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + \log y = \frac { 1 } { u } \frac { d u } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad u \left[ \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + \log y \right] = \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad y ^ { x } \left[ \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + \log y \right] = \frac { d u } { d x }{/tex} ........(v) [ From Eq(ii)] Therefore,on substituting the values of {tex}\frac { d v } { d x } \text { and } \frac { d u } { d x }{/tex} from Eqs. (iv) and (v) respectively in Eq. (iii), we get {tex}x ^ { y } \left( \frac { y } { x } + \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } \right) + y ^ { x } \left( \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + \log y \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x ^ { y } \frac { y } { x } + x ^ { y } \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } {/tex} {tex}+ y ^ { x } \cdot \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + y ^ { x } \log y = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x ^ { y } \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } + y ^ { x } \frac { x } { y } \cdot \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} {tex}= - x ^ { y } \frac { y } { x } - y ^ { x } \log y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d y } { d x } \left[ x ^ { y } \log x + y ^ { x } \cdot \frac { x } { y } \right]{/tex} {tex}= - x ^ { y } \cdot \frac { y } { x } - y ^ { x } \log y{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { - x ^ { y - 1 } \cdot y - y ^ { x } \log y } { x ^ { y } \log x + y ^ { x - 1 } \cdot x }{/tex}
solution: We have, x y + y x = a b .........(i) Let x y = v and y x = u......(ii) Therefore,on putting these values in Eq. (i), we get, v + u = a b Therefore,on differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get, {tex}\frac { d v } { d x } + \frac { d u } { d x } = 0{/tex} ........(iii) Now consider, x y = v [ from Eq.(ii)] Therefore,on taking log both sides, we get, log x y = logv {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log x = log v Therefore,on differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get, {tex}y \cdot \frac { 1 } { x } + \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { 1 } { v } \frac { d v } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow v \left( \frac { y } { x } + \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } \right) = \frac { d v } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d v } { d x } = x ^ { y } \left( \frac { y } { x } + \log x \frac { d y } { d x } \right){/tex} .........(iv) [ From Eq.(ii)] Also, y x = u [From Eq(ii)] Therefore,on taking log both sides, we get, log y x = log u {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x log y = log u Therefore,on differentiating both sides w.r.t. 'x', we get, {tex}x \cdot \frac { 1 } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + 1 \cdot \log y = \frac { 1 } { u } \frac { d u } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + \log y = \frac { 1 } { u } \frac { d u } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad u \left[ \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + \log y \right] = \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad y ^ { x } \left[ \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + \log y \right] = \frac { d u } { d x }{/tex} ........(v) [ From Eq(ii)] Therefore,on substituting the values of {tex}\frac { d v } { d x } \text { and } \frac { d u } { d x }{/tex} from Eqs. (iv) and (v) respectively in Eq. (iii), we get {tex}x ^ { y } \left( \frac { y } { x } + \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } \right) + y ^ { x } \left( \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + \log y \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x ^ { y } \frac { y } { x } + x ^ { y } \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } {/tex} {tex}+ y ^ { x } \cdot \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + y ^ { x } \log y = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x ^ { y } \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } + y ^ { x } \frac { x } { y } \cdot \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex} {tex}= - x ^ { y } \frac { y } { x } - y ^ { x } \log y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d y } { d x } \left[ x ^ { y } \log x + y ^ { x } \cdot \frac { x } { y } \right]{/tex} {tex}= - x ^ { y } \cdot \frac { y } { x } - y ^ { x } \log y{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { - x ^ { y - 1 } \cdot y - y ^ { x } \log y } { x ^ { y } \log x + y ^ { x - 1 } \cdot x }{/tex}
solution_html:
We have,  xy + yx = ab.........(i)
Let xy = v and yx = u......(ii)
Therefore,on putting these values in Eq. (i), we get,
v + u = ab

Therefore,on differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get,
{tex}\frac { d v } { d x } + \frac { d u } { d x } = 0{/tex}........(iii)
Now consider, xy = v [ from Eq.(ii)]
Therefore,on taking log both sides, we get,
log xy = logv
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log x = log v
Therefore,on differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get,
{tex}y \cdot \frac { 1 } { x } + \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { 1 } { v } \frac { d v } { d x }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow v \left( \frac { y } { x } + \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } \right) = \frac { d v } { d x }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d v } { d x } = x ^ { y } \left( \frac { y } { x } + \log x \frac { d y } { d x } \right){/tex}.........(iv) [ From Eq.(ii)]
Also, yx = u [From Eq(ii)]
Therefore,on taking log both sides, we get,
log yx = log u {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x log y = log u
Therefore,on differentiating both sides w.r.t. 'x', we get,
{tex}x \cdot \frac { 1 } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + 1 \cdot \log y = \frac { 1 } { u } \frac { d u } { d x }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + \log y = \frac { 1 } { u } \frac { d u } { d x }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad u \left[ \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + \log y \right] = \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad y ^ { x } \left[ \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + \log y \right] = \frac { d u } { d x }{/tex}........(v) [ From Eq(ii)]
Therefore,on substituting the values of {tex}\frac { d v } { d x } \text { and } \frac { d u } { d x }{/tex} from Eqs. (iv) and (v) respectively in Eq. (iii), we get
{tex}x ^ { y } \left( \frac { y } { x } + \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } \right) + y ^ { x } \left( \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + \log y \right) = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x ^ { y } \frac { y } { x } + x ^ { y } \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } {/tex}{tex}+ y ^ { x } \cdot \frac { x } { y } \frac { d y } { d x } + y ^ { x } \log y = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x ^ { y } \log x \cdot \frac { d y } { d x } + y ^ { x } \frac { x } { y } \cdot \frac { d y } { d x }{/tex}{tex}= - x ^ { y } \frac { y } { x } - y ^ { x } \log y{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d y } { d x } \left[ x ^ { y } \log x + y ^ { x } \cdot \frac { x } { y } \right]{/tex}{tex}= - x ^ { y } \cdot \frac { y } { x } - y ^ { x } \log y{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { - x ^ { y - 1 } \cdot y - y ^ { x } \log y } { x ^ { y } \log x + y ^ { x - 1 } \cdot x }{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}, \text { if } x^{y} \cdot y^{x}=x^{x}{/tex}.
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}, \text { if } x^{y} \cdot y^{x}=x^{x}{/tex} .
explanation: x y ⋅ y x = x x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log x + x log y = x log x (taking log both sides) differentiate both sides w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(y \cdot \frac{1}{x}+\log x \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}\right){/tex} + {tex}\left(x \cdot \frac{1}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}+\log y \cdot 1\right){/tex} = {tex}x \cdot \frac{1}{x}+\log x \cdot 1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{y}{x}+\log \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-1{/tex} = {tex}-\left(\log x+\frac{x}{y}\right) \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1-\frac{y}{x}-\log \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{\log x+\frac{x}{y}}{/tex} or {tex}\frac{y}{x}\left[\frac{x+x \log x-y-x \log y}{y \log x+x}\right]{/tex}
solution: x y ⋅ y x = x x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log x + x log y = x log x (taking log both sides) differentiate both sides w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(y \cdot \frac{1}{x}+\log x \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}\right){/tex} + {tex}\left(x \cdot \frac{1}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}+\log y \cdot 1\right){/tex} = {tex}x \cdot \frac{1}{x}+\log x \cdot 1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{y}{x}+\log \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-1{/tex} = {tex}-\left(\log x+\frac{x}{y}\right) \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1-\frac{y}{x}-\log \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{\log x+\frac{x}{y}}{/tex} or {tex}\frac{y}{x}\left[\frac{x+x \log x-y-x \log y}{y \log x+x}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
xy ⋅ yx = xx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log x + x log y = x log x (taking log both sides)
differentiate both sides w.r.t. x, we get
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left(y \cdot \frac{1}{x}+\log x \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}\right){/tex}+{tex}\left(x \cdot \frac{1}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}+\log y \cdot 1\right){/tex}={tex}x \cdot \frac{1}{x}+\log x \cdot 1{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{y}{x}+\log \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-1{/tex} ={tex}-\left(\log x+\frac{x}{y}\right) \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}={tex}\frac{1-\frac{y}{x}-\log \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{\log x+\frac{x}{y}}{/tex}
or
{tex}\frac{y}{x}\left[\frac{x+x \log x-y-x \log y}{y \log x+x}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}{\left( {x - a} \right)^2} + {\left( {y - b} \right)^2} = {c^2}{/tex} for some c > 0 prove that {tex}\frac{{{{\left[ {1 + {{\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right)}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}}}{/tex} is a constant independent of a and b.
question_text: If {tex}{\left( {x - a} \right)^2} + {\left( {y - b} \right)^2} = {c^2}{/tex} for some c > 0 prove that {tex}\frac{{{{\left[ {1 + {{\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right)}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}}}{/tex} is a constant independent of a and b.
explanation: Given: {tex}{\left( {x - a} \right)^2} + {\left( {y - b} \right)^2} = {c^2},{/tex} ……….(i) {tex}\therefore 2\left( {x - a} \right) + 2\left( {y - b} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2\left( {x - a} \right) = - 2\left( {y - b} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \left( {\frac{{x - a}}{{y - b}}} \right){/tex} ……….(ii) Again {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right).1 - \left( {x - a} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right).1 - \left( {x - a} \right)\left( {\frac{{ - \left( {x - a} \right)}}{{y - b}}} \right)} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} [From eq. (ii) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right) + \left( {\frac{{{{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}}}{{y - b}}} \right)} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - \left[ {{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - {c^2}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{/tex} ……….(iii) [using (i)] Putting values of {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}{/tex} in the given expression, {tex}\frac{{{{\left[ {1 + {{\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right)}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{{{\left[ {1 + {{\frac{{\left( {x - a} \right)}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{{ - {c^2}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{{\left[ {{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}} \times \frac{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{{ - {c^2}}} = \frac{{{{\left( {{c^2}} \right)}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{ - {c^2}}} = - c{/tex} which is a constant and is independent of a and b.
solution: Given: {tex}{\left( {x - a} \right)^2} + {\left( {y - b} \right)^2} = {c^2},{/tex} ……….(i) {tex}\therefore 2\left( {x - a} \right) + 2\left( {y - b} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2\left( {x - a} \right) = - 2\left( {y - b} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \left( {\frac{{x - a}}{{y - b}}} \right){/tex} ……….(ii) Again {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right).1 - \left( {x - a} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right).1 - \left( {x - a} \right)\left( {\frac{{ - \left( {x - a} \right)}}{{y - b}}} \right)} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} [From eq. (ii) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right) + \left( {\frac{{{{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}}}{{y - b}}} \right)} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - \left[ {{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - {c^2}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{/tex} ……….(iii) [using (i)] Putting values of {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}{/tex} in the given expression, {tex}\frac{{{{\left[ {1 + {{\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right)}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{{{\left[ {1 + {{\frac{{\left( {x - a} \right)}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{{ - {c^2}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{{\left[ {{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}} \times \frac{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{{ - {c^2}}} = \frac{{{{\left( {{c^2}} \right)}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{ - {c^2}}} = - c{/tex} which is a constant and is independent of a and b.
solution_html:
Given: {tex}{\left( {x - a} \right)^2} + {\left( {y - b} \right)^2} = {c^2},{/tex}……….(i)

{tex}\therefore 2\left( {x - a} \right) + 2\left( {y - b} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 0{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 2\left( {x - a} \right) = - 2\left( {y - b} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \left( {\frac{{x - a}}{{y - b}}} \right){/tex} ……….(ii)

Again {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right).1 - \left( {x - a} \right)\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} 

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right).1 - \left( {x - a} \right)\left( {\frac{{ - \left( {x - a} \right)}}{{y - b}}} \right)} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} [From eq. (ii)

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{{ - \left[ {\left( {y - b} \right) + \left( {\frac{{{{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}}}{{y - b}}} \right)} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - \left[ {{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}} \right]}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - {c^2}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{/tex} ……….(iii)  [using (i)]

Putting values of {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}{/tex} in the given expression,

{tex}\frac{{{{\left[ {1 + {{\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right)}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}}}{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{{{{\left[ {1 + {{\frac{{\left( {x - a} \right)}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2}}}}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{{ - {c^2}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{{\left[ {{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {x - a} \right)}^2}} \right]}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}} \times \frac{{{{\left( {y - b} \right)}^3}}}{{ - {c^2}}} = \frac{{{{\left( {{c^2}} \right)}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{ - {c^2}}} = - c{/tex}

which is a constant and is independent of a and b.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}x\sqrt {1 + y} + y\sqrt {1 + x} = 0{/tex}  for -1<x<1 prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \frac{1}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}
question_text: If {tex}x\sqrt {1 + y} + y\sqrt {1 + x} = 0{/tex} for -1<x<1 prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \frac{1}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}
explanation: {tex}x\sqrt {1 + y} + y\sqrt {1 + x} = 0{/tex} {tex}x\sqrt {1 + y} = - y\sqrt {1 + x} {/tex} Squaring both sides, {tex}{x^2}\left( {1 + y} \right) = {y^2}\left( {1 + x} \right){/tex} {tex}{x^2} + {x^2}y = {y^2} + x{y^2}{/tex} {tex}{x^2} - {y^2} + {x^2}y - x{y^2} = 0{/tex} {tex}\left( {x - y} \right)\left( {x + y} \right) + xy\left( {x - y} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\left( {x - y} \right)\left[ {x + y + xy} \right] = 0{/tex} {tex}x + y + xy = 0{/tex} {tex}y\left( {1 + x} \right) = - x{/tex} {tex}y = \frac{{ - x}}{{1 + x}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \left[ {\frac{{\left( {1 + x} \right)\left( 1 \right) - \left( x \right)\left( 1 \right)}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= - \left[ {\frac{{1 + x - x}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}
solution: {tex}x\sqrt {1 + y} + y\sqrt {1 + x} = 0{/tex} {tex}x\sqrt {1 + y} = - y\sqrt {1 + x} {/tex} Squaring both sides, {tex}{x^2}\left( {1 + y} \right) = {y^2}\left( {1 + x} \right){/tex} {tex}{x^2} + {x^2}y = {y^2} + x{y^2}{/tex} {tex}{x^2} - {y^2} + {x^2}y - x{y^2} = 0{/tex} {tex}\left( {x - y} \right)\left( {x + y} \right) + xy\left( {x - y} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\left( {x - y} \right)\left[ {x + y + xy} \right] = 0{/tex} {tex}x + y + xy = 0{/tex} {tex}y\left( {1 + x} \right) = - x{/tex} {tex}y = \frac{{ - x}}{{1 + x}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \left[ {\frac{{\left( {1 + x} \right)\left( 1 \right) - \left( x \right)\left( 1 \right)}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= - \left[ {\frac{{1 + x - x}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}x\sqrt {1 + y} + y\sqrt {1 + x} = 0{/tex}

{tex}x\sqrt {1 + y} = - y\sqrt {1 + x} {/tex}

Squaring both sides,

{tex}{x^2}\left( {1 + y} \right) = {y^2}\left( {1 + x} \right){/tex}

{tex}{x^2} + {x^2}y = {y^2} + x{y^2}{/tex}

{tex}{x^2} - {y^2} + {x^2}y - x{y^2} = 0{/tex}

{tex}\left( {x - y} \right)\left( {x + y} \right) + xy\left( {x - y} \right) = 0{/tex}

{tex}\left( {x - y} \right)\left[ {x + y + xy} \right] = 0{/tex}

{tex}x + y + xy = 0{/tex}

{tex}y\left( {1 + x} \right) = - x{/tex}

{tex}y = \frac{{ - x}}{{1 + x}}{/tex}

{tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \left[ {\frac{{\left( {1 + x} \right)\left( 1 \right) - \left( x \right)\left( 1 \right)}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}} \right]{/tex}{tex}= - \left[ {\frac{{1 + x - x}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{{{\left( {1 + x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If x = cos t + log tan {tex}\left(\frac{t}{2}\right){/tex}, y = sint, then find the value of {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} at t = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
question_text: If x = cos t + log tan {tex}\left(\frac{t}{2}\right){/tex} , y = sint, then find the value of {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} at t = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
explanation: Given: x = cos t + log tan {tex}\frac{t}{2}{/tex} Differentiating w.r.t. t, we get {tex}\frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\tan \left(\frac{t}{2}\right)} \cdot \frac{1}{2} \sec ^{2} \frac{t}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{\cos \left(\frac{t}{2}\right)}{2 \cdot \sin \left(\frac{t}{2}\right)} \cdot \frac{1}{\cos ^{2}\left(\frac{t}{2}\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{2 \sin \left(\frac{t}{2}\right) \cos \left(\frac{t}{2}\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\sin t}{/tex} {tex}\left[\because sin A=2 \sin \frac{A}{2} \cos \frac{A}{2}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{-\sin ^{2} t+1}{\sin t}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{\cos ^{2} t}{\sin t}{/tex} ...(i) Now y = sin t (Given) Differentiating w.r.t. t, we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}=\cos t{/tex} Now, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y}{d t} \times \frac{d t}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\cos t \times \frac{\sin t}{\cos ^{2} t}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\tan t{/tex} Again, Differentiating both sides w.r.t., x, we get {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\sec ^{2} t \frac{d t}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\sec ^{2} t \times \frac{\sin t}{\cos ^{2} t}{/tex} [using (i)] {tex}\left.\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}\right|_{t=\frac{\pi}{4}}=\sec ^{2} \pi / 4 \cdot \frac{\sin \pi / 4}{\cos ^{2} \pi / 4}{/tex} {tex}=(\sqrt{2})^{2} \cdot \frac{\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}{\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{2 \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}{\frac{1}{2}}=\frac{4}{\sqrt{2}}=2 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
solution: Given: x = cos t + log tan {tex}\frac{t}{2}{/tex} Differentiating w.r.t. t, we get {tex}\frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\tan \left(\frac{t}{2}\right)} \cdot \frac{1}{2} \sec ^{2} \frac{t}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{\cos \left(\frac{t}{2}\right)}{2 \cdot \sin \left(\frac{t}{2}\right)} \cdot \frac{1}{\cos ^{2}\left(\frac{t}{2}\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{2 \sin \left(\frac{t}{2}\right) \cos \left(\frac{t}{2}\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\sin t}{/tex} {tex}\left[\because sin A=2 \sin \frac{A}{2} \cos \frac{A}{2}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{-\sin ^{2} t+1}{\sin t}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{\cos ^{2} t}{\sin t}{/tex} ...(i) Now y = sin t (Given) Differentiating w.r.t. t, we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}=\cos t{/tex} Now, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y}{d t} \times \frac{d t}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\cos t \times \frac{\sin t}{\cos ^{2} t}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\tan t{/tex} Again, Differentiating both sides w.r.t., x, we get {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\sec ^{2} t \frac{d t}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\sec ^{2} t \times \frac{\sin t}{\cos ^{2} t}{/tex} [using (i)] {tex}\left.\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}\right|_{t=\frac{\pi}{4}}=\sec ^{2} \pi / 4 \cdot \frac{\sin \pi / 4}{\cos ^{2} \pi / 4}{/tex} {tex}=(\sqrt{2})^{2} \cdot \frac{\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}{\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{2 \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}{\frac{1}{2}}=\frac{4}{\sqrt{2}}=2 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: x = cos t + log tan{tex}\frac{t}{2}{/tex}
Differentiating w.r.t. t, we get
{tex}\frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\tan \left(\frac{t}{2}\right)} \cdot \frac{1}{2} \sec ^{2} \frac{t}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{\cos \left(\frac{t}{2}\right)}{2 \cdot \sin \left(\frac{t}{2}\right)} \cdot \frac{1}{\cos ^{2}\left(\frac{t}{2}\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{2 \sin \left(\frac{t}{2}\right) \cos \left(\frac{t}{2}\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d t}=-\sin t+\frac{1}{\sin t}{/tex} {tex}\left[\because sin A=2 \sin \frac{A}{2} \cos \frac{A}{2}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{-\sin ^{2} t+1}{\sin t}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{\cos ^{2} t}{\sin t}{/tex} ...(i)
Now y = sin t (Given)
Differentiating w.r.t. t, we get
{tex}\frac{d y}{d t}=\cos t{/tex}
Now,{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y}{d t} \times \frac{d t}{d x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\cos t \times \frac{\sin t}{\cos ^{2} t}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\tan t{/tex}
Again, Differentiating both sides w.r.t., x, we get
{tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\sec ^{2} t \frac{d t}{d x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\sec ^{2} t \times \frac{\sin t}{\cos ^{2} t}{/tex} [using (i)]
{tex}\left.\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}\right|_{t=\frac{\pi}{4}}=\sec ^{2} \pi / 4 \cdot \frac{\sin \pi / 4}{\cos ^{2} \pi / 4}{/tex}
{tex}=(\sqrt{2})^{2} \cdot \frac{\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}{\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2 \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}{\frac{1}{2}}=\frac{4}{\sqrt{2}}=2 \sqrt{2}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If log(x2 + y2) = 2 tan-1{tex}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex}, show that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex}.
question_text: If log(x 2 + y 2 ) = 2 tan -1 {tex}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} , show that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex} .
explanation: Differentiating both sides of the given relation with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{d}{d x}\left\{\log \left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)\right\}=2 \frac{d}{d x}\left\{\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)\right\}q{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{x^{2}+y^{2}} \times \frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)=2 \times \frac{1}{1+(y / x)^{2}} \times \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{x^{2}+y^{2}}\left\{\frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(y^{2}\right)\right\}=2 \times \frac{x^{2}}{x^{2}+y^{2}}\left\{\frac{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y \times 1}{x^{2}}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{x^{2}+y^{2}}\left\{2 x+2 y \frac{d y}{d x}\right\}=\frac{2}{x^{2}+y^{2}}\left\{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2\left\{x+y \frac{d y}{d x}\right\}=2\left\{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x+y \frac{d y}{d x}=x \frac{d y}{d x}-y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}(y-x)=-(x+y){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex} LHS=RHS Hence Proved.
solution: Differentiating both sides of the given relation with respect to x, we get {tex}\frac{d}{d x}\left\{\log \left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)\right\}=2 \frac{d}{d x}\left\{\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)\right\}q{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{x^{2}+y^{2}} \times \frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)=2 \times \frac{1}{1+(y / x)^{2}} \times \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{x^{2}+y^{2}}\left\{\frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(y^{2}\right)\right\}=2 \times \frac{x^{2}}{x^{2}+y^{2}}\left\{\frac{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y \times 1}{x^{2}}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{x^{2}+y^{2}}\left\{2 x+2 y \frac{d y}{d x}\right\}=\frac{2}{x^{2}+y^{2}}\left\{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2\left\{x+y \frac{d y}{d x}\right\}=2\left\{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x+y \frac{d y}{d x}=x \frac{d y}{d x}-y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}(y-x)=-(x+y){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex} LHS=RHS Hence Proved.
solution_html:
Differentiating both sides of the given relation with respect to x, we get
{tex}\frac{d}{d x}\left\{\log \left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)\right\}=2 \frac{d}{d x}\left\{\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)\right\}q{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{x^{2}+y^{2}} \times \frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)=2 \times \frac{1}{1+(y / x)^{2}} \times \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{x^{2}+y^{2}}\left\{\frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(y^{2}\right)\right\}=2 \times \frac{x^{2}}{x^{2}+y^{2}}\left\{\frac{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y \times 1}{x^{2}}\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{x^{2}+y^{2}}\left\{2 x+2 y \frac{d y}{d x}\right\}=\frac{2}{x^{2}+y^{2}}\left\{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2\left\{x+y \frac{d y}{d x}\right\}=2\left\{x \frac{d y}{d x}-y\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x+y \frac{d y}{d x}=x \frac{d y}{d x}-y{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}(y-x)=-(x+y){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex}
LHS=RHS
Hence Proved.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Differentiate {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}-\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right\}{/tex} with respect to cos-1x2.
question_text: Differentiate {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}-\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right\}{/tex} with respect to cos -1 x 2 .
explanation: Let, {tex}u =\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}-\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right\}{/tex} Put, x 2 = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} , we get {tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\sqrt{1+\cos \theta}-\sqrt{1-\cos \theta}}{\sqrt{1+\cos \theta}+\sqrt{1-\cos \theta}}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\sqrt{2 \cos ^{2} \theta / 2}-\sqrt{2 \sin ^{2} \theta / 2}}{\sqrt{2 \cos ^{2} \theta / 2}+\sqrt{2 \sin ^{2} \theta / 2}}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\cos \theta / 2-\sin \theta / 2}{\cos \theta / 2+\sin \theta / 2}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{1-\tan \theta / 2}{1+\tan \theta / 2}\right\}{/tex} [Dividing numerator and denominator by {tex}\cos \frac{\theta}{2}{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{\theta}{2}\right)\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow u=\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2} \theta=\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x^{2}{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} x2 = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = cos -1 x 2 ] {tex}\therefore \frac{d u}{d x}=-\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}}=\frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}}{/tex} Now, {tex}v=\cos ^{-1} x^{2} \Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d u}{d v}=\frac{d u / d x}{d v / d x}=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
solution: Let, {tex}u =\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}-\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right\}{/tex} Put, x 2 = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} , we get {tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\sqrt{1+\cos \theta}-\sqrt{1-\cos \theta}}{\sqrt{1+\cos \theta}+\sqrt{1-\cos \theta}}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\sqrt{2 \cos ^{2} \theta / 2}-\sqrt{2 \sin ^{2} \theta / 2}}{\sqrt{2 \cos ^{2} \theta / 2}+\sqrt{2 \sin ^{2} \theta / 2}}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\cos \theta / 2-\sin \theta / 2}{\cos \theta / 2+\sin \theta / 2}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{1-\tan \theta / 2}{1+\tan \theta / 2}\right\}{/tex} [Dividing numerator and denominator by {tex}\cos \frac{\theta}{2}{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{\theta}{2}\right)\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow u=\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2} \theta=\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x^{2}{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} x2 = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = cos -1 x 2 ] {tex}\therefore \frac{d u}{d x}=-\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}}=\frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}}{/tex} Now, {tex}v=\cos ^{-1} x^{2} \Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d u}{d v}=\frac{d u / d x}{d v / d x}=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let, {tex}u =\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}-\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right\}{/tex}
Put, x2 = cos{tex}\theta{/tex}, we get
{tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\sqrt{1+\cos \theta}-\sqrt{1-\cos \theta}}{\sqrt{1+\cos \theta}+\sqrt{1-\cos \theta}}\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\sqrt{2 \cos ^{2} \theta / 2}-\sqrt{2 \sin ^{2} \theta / 2}}{\sqrt{2 \cos ^{2} \theta / 2}+\sqrt{2 \sin ^{2} \theta / 2}}\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\cos \theta / 2-\sin \theta / 2}{\cos \theta / 2+\sin \theta / 2}\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{1-\tan \theta / 2}{1+\tan \theta / 2}\right\}{/tex} [Dividing numerator and denominator by {tex}\cos \frac{\theta}{2}{/tex}]
{tex}\Rightarrow u=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{\theta}{2}\right)\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow u=\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2} \theta=\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x^{2}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} x2 = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = cos-1x2]
{tex}\therefore \frac{d u}{d x}=-\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}}=\frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}}{/tex}
Now, {tex}v=\cos ^{-1} x^{2} \Rightarrow \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{d u}{d v}=\frac{d u / d x}{d v / d x}=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If y = sin-1 x + cos-1 x, find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
question_text: If y = sin -1 x + cos -1 x, find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
explanation: y = sin -1 x + cos -1 x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} x\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(\cos ^{-1} x\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} = 0 Therefore, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = 0
solution: y = sin -1 x + cos -1 x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} x\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(\cos ^{-1} x\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} = 0 Therefore, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = 0
solution_html:
y = sin-1x + cos-1x
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} x\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(\cos ^{-1} x\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex}
= 0
Therefore, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = 0

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If  y = log (cos ex), then find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}.
question_text: If y = log (cos e x ), then find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} .
explanation: We have, y = log (cos e x ), {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-\sin e^{x}}{\cos e^{x}} \cdot e^{x}{/tex} or {tex}-e^{x} \cdot \tan e^{x}{/tex} .
solution: We have, y = log (cos e x ), {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-\sin e^{x}}{\cos e^{x}} \cdot e^{x}{/tex} or {tex}-e^{x} \cdot \tan e^{x}{/tex} .
solution_html:
We have, y = log (cos ex),
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-\sin e^{x}}{\cos e^{x}} \cdot e^{x}{/tex} or {tex}-e^{x} \cdot \tan e^{x}{/tex}.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If y = sin-1 x + cos-1 x, find {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex}
question_text: If y = sin -1 x + cos -1 x, find {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex}
explanation: y = sin -1 x + cos -1 x Differentiating both sides {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{d}{dx}{/tex} (sin -1 x + cos -1 x) = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} + {tex}\left[\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} =0 OR y = sin -1 x + cos -1 x = {tex}\frac{\mathrm\pi}2{/tex} Differentiating both sides {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = 0
solution: y = sin -1 x + cos -1 x Differentiating both sides {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{d}{dx}{/tex} (sin -1 x + cos -1 x) = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} + {tex}\left[\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} =0 OR y = sin -1 x + cos -1 x = {tex}\frac{\mathrm\pi}2{/tex} Differentiating both sides {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = 0
solution_html:
y = sin-1 x + cos-1 x
Differentiating both sides
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{d}{dx}{/tex} (sin-1 x + cos-1 x)
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} + {tex}\left[\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} =0
OR
y = sin-1 x + cos-1 x = {tex}\frac{\mathrm\pi}2{/tex} 
Differentiating both sides
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = 0

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If y = (x)cosx + (cosx)sinx, find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}.
question_text: If y = (x) cosx + (cosx) sinx , find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} .
explanation: Let u = x cosx , v = (cosx) sinx y = u + v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} ........ (i) log u = cosx logx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{u} \frac{d u}{d x}=\frac{\cos x}{x}{/tex} - sin x log x {tex}\frac{du}{dx}{/tex} = x cosx {tex}\left(\frac{\cos x}{x}-\sin x \log x\right){/tex} .....(ii) log v = sin x log cos x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{v} \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = - sin x tan x + cos x log cos x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = (cos x) sinx (-sin x tan x + cos x log cos x).......(iii) So, substituting (ii) and (iii) in (i),we get {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = x cosx {tex}\left(\frac{\cos x}{x}-\sin x \log x\right){/tex} + (cos x) sinx (-sin x tan x + cos x log cos x)
solution: Let u = x cosx , v = (cosx) sinx y = u + v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} ........ (i) log u = cosx logx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{u} \frac{d u}{d x}=\frac{\cos x}{x}{/tex} - sin x log x {tex}\frac{du}{dx}{/tex} = x cosx {tex}\left(\frac{\cos x}{x}-\sin x \log x\right){/tex} .....(ii) log v = sin x log cos x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{v} \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = - sin x tan x + cos x log cos x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = (cos x) sinx (-sin x tan x + cos x log cos x).......(iii) So, substituting (ii) and (iii) in (i),we get {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = x cosx {tex}\left(\frac{\cos x}{x}-\sin x \log x\right){/tex} + (cos x) sinx (-sin x tan x + cos x log cos x)
solution_html:
Let u = xcosx, v = (cosx)sinx
y = u + v
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d u}{d x}+\frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} ........ (i)
log u = cosx logx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{u} \frac{d u}{d x}=\frac{\cos x}{x}{/tex} - sin x log x
{tex}\frac{du}{dx}{/tex} = xcosx {tex}\left(\frac{\cos x}{x}-\sin x \log x\right){/tex} .....(ii)
log v = sin x log cos x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{v} \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex}= - sin x tan x + cos x log cos x
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = (cos x)sinx (-sin x tan x + cos x log cos x).......(iii)
So, substituting (ii) and (iii) in (i),we get

 {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = xcosx {tex}\left(\frac{\cos x}{x}-\sin x \log x\right){/tex} + (cos x)sinx (-sin x tan x + cos x log cos x)

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If y = (sin–1x)2, prove that (1 – x2) {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-x \frac{d y}{d x}-2=0{/tex}
question_text: If y = (sin –1 x) 2 , prove that (1 – x 2 ) {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-x \frac{d y}{d x}-2=0{/tex}
explanation: We seek to show that {tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right) \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-x \frac{d y}{d x}-2{/tex} = 0 where y = (sin -1 x) 2 Using the result: {tex}\frac{d}{d x}(sin^{-1} x){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} In conjunction with the chain rule, then differentiating y = (sin-1 x) 2 w.r.t. x we have: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{2sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} And differentiating a second time, in conjunction with the quotient rule, we have: {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(\sqrt{1-x^{2}})\left(\frac{2}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)-\left(\frac{\frac{1}{2}(-2 x)}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)(2 sin^{-1} x)}{(\sqrt{1-x^{2}})^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2+\frac{2 x sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}}{1-x^{2}}{/tex} , considering the LHS of the given expression: LHS = {tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right) \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-x \frac{d y}{d x}-2{/tex} = {tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right)\left\{\frac{2+\frac{2 x sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}}{1-x^{2}}\right\}{/tex} - {tex}x\left\{\frac{2 sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right\}{/tex} - 2 = 2 + {tex}\frac{2 x sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{2 x sin^{-1}x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} - 2 = 0
solution: We seek to show that {tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right) \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-x \frac{d y}{d x}-2{/tex} = 0 where y = (sin -1 x) 2 Using the result: {tex}\frac{d}{d x}(sin^{-1} x){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} In conjunction with the chain rule, then differentiating y = (sin-1 x) 2 w.r.t. x we have: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{2sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} And differentiating a second time, in conjunction with the quotient rule, we have: {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(\sqrt{1-x^{2}})\left(\frac{2}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)-\left(\frac{\frac{1}{2}(-2 x)}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)(2 sin^{-1} x)}{(\sqrt{1-x^{2}})^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2+\frac{2 x sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}}{1-x^{2}}{/tex} , considering the LHS of the given expression: LHS = {tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right) \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-x \frac{d y}{d x}-2{/tex} = {tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right)\left\{\frac{2+\frac{2 x sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}}{1-x^{2}}\right\}{/tex} - {tex}x\left\{\frac{2 sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right\}{/tex} - 2 = 2 + {tex}\frac{2 x sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{2 x sin^{-1}x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} - 2 = 0
solution_html:
We seek to show that 
{tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right) \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-x \frac{d y}{d x}-2{/tex} = 0 where y = (sin-1 x)2 
Using the result:
{tex}\frac{d}{d x}(sin^{-1} x){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} 
In conjunction with the chain rule, then differentiating y = (sin-1 x)2 w.r.t. x we have:
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{2sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} 
And differentiating a second time, in conjunction with the quotient rule, we have:
{tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(\sqrt{1-x^{2}})\left(\frac{2}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)-\left(\frac{\frac{1}{2}(-2 x)}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)(2 sin^{-1} x)}{(\sqrt{1-x^{2}})^{2}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{2+\frac{2 x sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}}{1-x^{2}}{/tex} 
, considering the LHS of the given expression:
LHS = {tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right) \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-x \frac{d y}{d x}-2{/tex} 
= {tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right)\left\{\frac{2+\frac{2 x sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}}{1-x^{2}}\right\}{/tex} - {tex}x\left\{\frac{2 sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right\}{/tex} - 2
= 2 + {tex}\frac{2 x sin^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{2 x sin^{-1}x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} - 2
= 0

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Differentiate {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{3 \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{x}^3}{1-3 \mathrm{x}^2},|\mathrm{x}|<\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex} w.r.t. {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex}.
question_text: Differentiate {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{3 \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{x}^3}{1-3 \mathrm{x}^2},|\mathrm{x}|<\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex} w.r.t. {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex} .
explanation: We have, tan -1 {tex}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right){/tex} let x = sin {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = sin -1 x , {tex}\sqrt{1-x^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{1- (sin^{-1}) ^{2} \theta}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{\cos ^{2} \theta}{/tex} = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} tan -1 {tex}\left(\frac{\sin \theta}{\cos \theta}\right){/tex} = tan -1 (tan {tex}\theta{/tex} ) {tex}\theta{/tex} = sin -1 x tan -1 {tex}\left(\frac{3 x-x^{3}}{1-3 x^{2}}\right){/tex} , |x| < {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex} = 3 tan -1 x {tex}\frac{d\left(\tan ^{-}\left(\frac{3 x-x^{3}}{1-3 x^{2}}\right)\right)}{dx\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{d\left(3 \tan ^{-1} x\right)}{d\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac{3 d\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)}{d x}}{\frac{d\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)}{d x}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3\left(\frac{1}{1+x^{2}}\right)}{\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3 \sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{1+x^2}{/tex} .
solution: We have, tan -1 {tex}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right){/tex} let x = sin {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = sin -1 x , {tex}\sqrt{1-x^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{1- (sin^{-1}) ^{2} \theta}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{\cos ^{2} \theta}{/tex} = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} tan -1 {tex}\left(\frac{\sin \theta}{\cos \theta}\right){/tex} = tan -1 (tan {tex}\theta{/tex} ) {tex}\theta{/tex} = sin -1 x tan -1 {tex}\left(\frac{3 x-x^{3}}{1-3 x^{2}}\right){/tex} , |x| < {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex} = 3 tan -1 x {tex}\frac{d\left(\tan ^{-}\left(\frac{3 x-x^{3}}{1-3 x^{2}}\right)\right)}{dx\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{d\left(3 \tan ^{-1} x\right)}{d\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac{3 d\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)}{d x}}{\frac{d\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)}{d x}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3\left(\frac{1}{1+x^{2}}\right)}{\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3 \sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{1+x^2}{/tex} .
solution_html:
We have,
tan-1 {tex}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right){/tex} 
let x = sin {tex}\theta{/tex} 
{tex}\theta{/tex} = sin-1 x , {tex}\sqrt{1-x^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{1- (sin^{-1}) ^{2} \theta}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{\cos ^{2} \theta}{/tex} = cos {tex}\theta{/tex}
tan-1 {tex}\left(\frac{\sin \theta}{\cos \theta}\right){/tex} 
= tan-1 (tan {tex}\theta{/tex})
{tex}\theta{/tex} = sin-1 x
tan-1 {tex}\left(\frac{3 x-x^{3}}{1-3 x^{2}}\right){/tex}, |x| < {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex} 
= 3 tan-1 x
{tex}\frac{d\left(\tan ^{-}\left(\frac{3 x-x^{3}}{1-3 x^{2}}\right)\right)}{dx\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{d\left(3 \tan ^{-1} x\right)}{d\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\frac{3 d\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)}{d x}}{\frac{d\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)}{d x}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{3\left(\frac{1}{1+x^{2}}\right)}{\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{3 \sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{1+x^2}{/tex}.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If (sin x)y = x + y, find {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex}
question_text: If (sin x) y = x + y, find {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex}
explanation: Given that, (sin x) y = x + y Taking log on both the sides, log(sin x) y = log (x + y) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log (sin x) = log (x + y) Differentiating both sides with respect to x using chain rule, {tex}\frac{d}{d x}\{y \log (\sin x)\}=\frac{d}{d x}\{\log (x+y)\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \frac{d}{d x} \log \sin x+\log \sin x \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{x+y} \frac{d}{d x}(x+y){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y}{\sin x} \frac{d}{d x}(\sin x)+\log \sin x \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{(x+y)}\left[1+\frac{d y}{d x}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y(\cos x)}{(\sin x)}+\log \sin x \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{(x+y)}+\frac{1}{(x+y)} \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}\left(\log \sin x-\frac{1}{x+y}\right)=\frac{1}{(x+y)}-y \cot x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}\left\{\frac{(x+y) \log \sin x-1}{(x+y)}\right\}=\frac{1-y(x+y) \cot x}{x+y}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\left\{\frac{1-y(x+y) \cot x}{(x+y) \log \sin x-1}\right\}{/tex}
solution: Given that, (sin x) y = x + y Taking log on both the sides, log(sin x) y = log (x + y) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log (sin x) = log (x + y) Differentiating both sides with respect to x using chain rule, {tex}\frac{d}{d x}\{y \log (\sin x)\}=\frac{d}{d x}\{\log (x+y)\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \frac{d}{d x} \log \sin x+\log \sin x \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{x+y} \frac{d}{d x}(x+y){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y}{\sin x} \frac{d}{d x}(\sin x)+\log \sin x \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{(x+y)}\left[1+\frac{d y}{d x}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y(\cos x)}{(\sin x)}+\log \sin x \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{(x+y)}+\frac{1}{(x+y)} \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}\left(\log \sin x-\frac{1}{x+y}\right)=\frac{1}{(x+y)}-y \cot x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}\left\{\frac{(x+y) \log \sin x-1}{(x+y)}\right\}=\frac{1-y(x+y) \cot x}{x+y}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\left\{\frac{1-y(x+y) \cot x}{(x+y) \log \sin x-1}\right\}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given that,  (sin x)y = x + y
Taking log on both the sides,
log(sin x)y = log (x + y)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log (sin x) = log (x + y)
Differentiating both sides with respect to x using chain rule,
{tex}\frac{d}{d x}\{y \log (\sin x)\}=\frac{d}{d x}\{\log (x+y)\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow y \frac{d}{d x} \log \sin x+\log \sin x \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{x+y} \frac{d}{d x}(x+y){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y}{\sin x} \frac{d}{d x}(\sin x)+\log \sin x \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{(x+y)}\left[1+\frac{d y}{d x}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y(\cos x)}{(\sin x)}+\log \sin x \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{(x+y)}+\frac{1}{(x+y)} \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}\left(\log \sin x-\frac{1}{x+y}\right)=\frac{1}{(x+y)}-y \cot x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}\left\{\frac{(x+y) \log \sin x-1}{(x+y)}\right\}=\frac{1-y(x+y) \cot x}{x+y}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\left\{\frac{1-y(x+y) \cot x}{(x+y) \log \sin x-1}\right\}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If xy - yx = ab, find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}.
question_text: If x y - y x = a b , find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} .
explanation: Given, x y - y x = a b {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}e^{y \log x}-e^{x \log y}=e^{b \log a}{/tex} On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{y \log x}\right)-\frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{x \log y}\right)=\frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{b \log a}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{e}^{y \log x} \frac{d}{d x}(y \log x){/tex} {tex}-\mathrm{e}^{x \log y} \frac{d}{d x}(x \log y)=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x^y\left\{\frac{d y}{d x} \times \log x+y \times \frac{1}{x}\right\}-y^x{/tex} {tex}\left\{1 \times \log y+x \times \frac{1}{y} \times \frac{d y}{d x}\right\}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left\{x^y \times \log x-y^x \times \frac{x}{y}\right\} \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} {tex}+\left\{x^y \times \frac{y}{x}-y^x \times \log y\right\}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left\{x^y \log x-x y^{x-1}\right\} \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} {tex}+\left\{y x^{y-1}-y^x \log y\right\}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\frac{d y}{d x}=\left\{\frac{y^x \log y-x^{y-1} y}{x^y \log x-y^{x-1} x}\right\}{/tex}
solution: Given, x y - y x = a b {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}e^{y \log x}-e^{x \log y}=e^{b \log a}{/tex} On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{y \log x}\right)-\frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{x \log y}\right)=\frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{b \log a}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{e}^{y \log x} \frac{d}{d x}(y \log x){/tex} {tex}-\mathrm{e}^{x \log y} \frac{d}{d x}(x \log y)=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x^y\left\{\frac{d y}{d x} \times \log x+y \times \frac{1}{x}\right\}-y^x{/tex} {tex}\left\{1 \times \log y+x \times \frac{1}{y} \times \frac{d y}{d x}\right\}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left\{x^y \times \log x-y^x \times \frac{x}{y}\right\} \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} {tex}+\left\{x^y \times \frac{y}{x}-y^x \times \log y\right\}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left\{x^y \log x-x y^{x-1}\right\} \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} {tex}+\left\{y x^{y-1}-y^x \log y\right\}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\frac{d y}{d x}=\left\{\frac{y^x \log y-x^{y-1} y}{x^y \log x-y^{x-1} x}\right\}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given, xy - yx = ab
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}e^{y \log x}-e^{x \log y}=e^{b \log a}{/tex}
On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get 
{tex}\frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{y \log x}\right)-\frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{x \log y}\right)=\frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{b \log a}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{e}^{y \log x} \frac{d}{d x}(y \log x){/tex} {tex}-\mathrm{e}^{x \log y} \frac{d}{d x}(x \log y)=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x^y\left\{\frac{d y}{d x} \times \log x+y \times \frac{1}{x}\right\}-y^x{/tex}{tex}\left\{1 \times \log y+x \times \frac{1}{y} \times \frac{d y}{d x}\right\}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left\{x^y \times \log x-y^x \times \frac{x}{y}\right\} \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}{tex}+\left\{x^y \times \frac{y}{x}-y^x \times \log y\right\}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left\{x^y \log x-x y^{x-1}\right\} \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}{tex}+\left\{y x^{y-1}-y^x \log y\right\}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\frac{d y}{d x}=\left\{\frac{y^x \log y-x^{y-1} y}{x^y \log x-y^{x-1} x}\right\}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If y = (sin-1x) 2, prove that {tex}\left(1-x^2\right) \frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{d} x^2}-x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}-2{/tex} = 0.
question_text: If y = (sin -1 x) 2 , prove that {tex}\left(1-x^2\right) \frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{d} x^2}-x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}-2{/tex} = 0.
explanation: {tex}y=\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y^{\prime}=2 \cdot \sin ^{-1} x \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt{1-x^2} \cdot y^{\prime}=2 \sin ^{-1} x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt{1-x^2} \cdot y^{\prime \prime}+y^{\prime} \cdot \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{1-x^2}}(-2 x){/tex} {tex}=\frac{2}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left(1-x^2\right) \cdot y^{\prime \prime}-x y^{\prime}=2{/tex} {tex}\left(1-x^2\right) \cdot \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}-x \frac{d y}{d x}-2=0{/tex}
solution: {tex}y=\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y^{\prime}=2 \cdot \sin ^{-1} x \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt{1-x^2} \cdot y^{\prime}=2 \sin ^{-1} x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt{1-x^2} \cdot y^{\prime \prime}+y^{\prime} \cdot \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{1-x^2}}(-2 x){/tex} {tex}=\frac{2}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left(1-x^2\right) \cdot y^{\prime \prime}-x y^{\prime}=2{/tex} {tex}\left(1-x^2\right) \cdot \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}-x \frac{d y}{d x}-2=0{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}y=\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)^2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow y^{\prime}=2 \cdot \sin ^{-1} x \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt{1-x^2} \cdot y^{\prime}=2 \sin ^{-1} x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sqrt{1-x^2} \cdot y^{\prime \prime}+y^{\prime} \cdot \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{1-x^2}}(-2 x){/tex}{tex}=\frac{2}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(1-x^2\right) \cdot y^{\prime \prime}-x y^{\prime}=2{/tex}
{tex}\left(1-x^2\right) \cdot \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}-x \frac{d y}{d x}-2=0{/tex}

question_html: If (sin x)y = x + y, find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
question_text: If (sin x) y = x + y, find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
explanation: (sin x) y = (x + y) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log sin x = log(x + y) differentiating w.r.t. x, we get {tex}y \cot x +\log \sin x \times \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{x+y}\left(1+\frac{d y}{d x}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\frac{1}{x+y}-y \cot x}{\log \sin x-\frac{1}{x+y}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1-y(x+y) \cot x}{(x+y) \log \sin x-1}{/tex}
solution: (sin x) y = (x + y) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log sin x = log(x + y) differentiating w.r.t. x, we get {tex}y \cot x +\log \sin x \times \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{x+y}\left(1+\frac{d y}{d x}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\frac{1}{x+y}-y \cot x}{\log \sin x-\frac{1}{x+y}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1-y(x+y) \cot x}{(x+y) \log \sin x-1}{/tex}
solution_html:
(sin x)y = (x + y) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log sin x = log(x + y)
differentiating w.r.t. x, we get
{tex}y \cot x +\log \sin x \times \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{x+y}\left(1+\frac{d y}{d x}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\frac{1}{x+y}-y \cot x}{\log \sin x-\frac{1}{x+y}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1-y(x+y) \cot x}{(x+y) \log \sin x-1}{/tex}

question_html: Find {tex}\frac {dy} {dx}{/tex}, if y = {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{2^{\mathrm{x}+1}}{1+4^{\mathrm{x}}}\right]{/tex}.
question_text: Find {tex}\frac {dy} {dx}{/tex} , if y = {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{2^{\mathrm{x}+1}}{1+4^{\mathrm{x}}}\right]{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 \cdot 2^x}{1+\left(2^x\right)^2}\right)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 t}{1+t^2}\right){/tex} , where t = 2 x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 2 tan -1 t {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\frac{2}{1+\mathrm{t}^2}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{dx}}=2^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \log 2{/tex} . {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{2}{1+\mathrm{t}^2} \cdot 2^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \log 2=\frac{2^{\mathrm{x}+1} \cdot \log 2}{1+4^{\mathrm{x}}}{/tex}
solution: {tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 \cdot 2^x}{1+\left(2^x\right)^2}\right)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 t}{1+t^2}\right){/tex} , where t = 2 x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 2 tan -1 t {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\frac{2}{1+\mathrm{t}^2}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{dx}}=2^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \log 2{/tex} . {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{2}{1+\mathrm{t}^2} \cdot 2^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \log 2=\frac{2^{\mathrm{x}+1} \cdot \log 2}{1+4^{\mathrm{x}}}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}y=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 \cdot 2^x}{1+\left(2^x\right)^2}\right)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 t}{1+t^2}\right){/tex}, where t = 2x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 2 tan-1 t
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\frac{2}{1+\mathrm{t}^2}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{dx}}=2^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \log 2{/tex}.
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{2}{1+\mathrm{t}^2} \cdot 2^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \log 2=\frac{2^{\mathrm{x}+1} \cdot \log 2}{1+4^{\mathrm{x}}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If (a + bx) ey/x = x, then prove that {tex}x^3 \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=\left(x \frac{d y}{d x}-y\right)^2{/tex}.
question_text: If (a + bx) e y/x = x, then prove that {tex}x^3 \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=\left(x \frac{d y}{d x}-y\right)^2{/tex} .
explanation: e y/x = {tex}\frac{x}{a+b x}{/tex} , taking log, on both sides, we get {tex}\frac {y} {x}{/tex} = log x - log (a + bx) Differentiating with respect to ‘x’ {tex}\frac{x \cdot y^{\prime}-y}{x^2}=\frac{1}{x}-\frac{b}{a+b x}=\frac{a}{(a+b x) x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x \cdot y^{\prime}-y=\frac{a x}{a+b x}{/tex} ...(i) Differentiating with respect to ‘x’ {tex}\Rightarrow x \cdot y^{\prime \prime}+y^{\prime}-y^{\prime}=\frac{(a+b x) \cdot a-a x \cdot b}{(a+b x)^2}=\left(\frac{a}{a+b x}\right)^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x \cdot y^{\prime \prime}=\left(\frac{a}{a+b x}\right)^2 \Rightarrow x^3 \cdot y^{\prime \prime}=\left(\frac{a x}{a+b x}\right)^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x^3 \frac{d^2 y}{\mathrm{dx}^2}=\left\{x \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}-\mathrm{y}\right\}^2{/tex} (Using (i))
solution: e y/x = {tex}\frac{x}{a+b x}{/tex} , taking log, on both sides, we get {tex}\frac {y} {x}{/tex} = log x - log (a + bx) Differentiating with respect to ‘x’ {tex}\frac{x \cdot y^{\prime}-y}{x^2}=\frac{1}{x}-\frac{b}{a+b x}=\frac{a}{(a+b x) x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x \cdot y^{\prime}-y=\frac{a x}{a+b x}{/tex} ...(i) Differentiating with respect to ‘x’ {tex}\Rightarrow x \cdot y^{\prime \prime}+y^{\prime}-y^{\prime}=\frac{(a+b x) \cdot a-a x \cdot b}{(a+b x)^2}=\left(\frac{a}{a+b x}\right)^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x \cdot y^{\prime \prime}=\left(\frac{a}{a+b x}\right)^2 \Rightarrow x^3 \cdot y^{\prime \prime}=\left(\frac{a x}{a+b x}\right)^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x^3 \frac{d^2 y}{\mathrm{dx}^2}=\left\{x \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}-\mathrm{y}\right\}^2{/tex} (Using (i))
solution_html:
ey/x = {tex}\frac{x}{a+b x}{/tex}, taking log, on both sides, we get {tex}\frac {y} {x}{/tex} = log x - log (a + bx)
Differentiating with respect to ‘x’
{tex}\frac{x \cdot y^{\prime}-y}{x^2}=\frac{1}{x}-\frac{b}{a+b x}=\frac{a}{(a+b x) x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x \cdot y^{\prime}-y=\frac{a x}{a+b x}{/tex} ...(i)
Differentiating with respect to ‘x’
{tex}\Rightarrow x \cdot y^{\prime \prime}+y^{\prime}-y^{\prime}=\frac{(a+b x) \cdot a-a x \cdot b}{(a+b x)^2}=\left(\frac{a}{a+b x}\right)^2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x \cdot y^{\prime \prime}=\left(\frac{a}{a+b x}\right)^2 \Rightarrow x^3 \cdot y^{\prime \prime}=\left(\frac{a x}{a+b x}\right)^2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x^3 \frac{d^2 y}{\mathrm{dx}^2}=\left\{x \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}-\mathrm{y}\right\}^2{/tex} (Using (i))

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Differentiate xsin x + (sin x)cos x with respect to x.
question_text: Differentiate x sin x + (sin x) cos x with respect to x.
explanation: Let, y = x sin x + (sin x) cos x = u + v {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}+\frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} u = xs in x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log u = sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=x^{\sin x}\left\{\cos x \cdot \log x+\frac{\sin x}{x}\right\}{/tex} v = (sin x) cos x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log v = cos x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (sin x) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} = (sin x) cos x {cos x {tex}\cdot{/tex} cot x - sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log sin x} {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=x^{\sin x}\left\{\cos x \cdot \log x+\frac{\sin x}{x}\right\}+(\sin x)^{\cos x}{/tex} {cos x {tex}\cdot{/tex} cot x - sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log sin x}
solution: Let, y = x sin x + (sin x) cos x = u + v {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}+\frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} u = xs in x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log u = sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=x^{\sin x}\left\{\cos x \cdot \log x+\frac{\sin x}{x}\right\}{/tex} v = (sin x) cos x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log v = cos x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (sin x) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} = (sin x) cos x {cos x {tex}\cdot{/tex} cot x - sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log sin x} {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=x^{\sin x}\left\{\cos x \cdot \log x+\frac{\sin x}{x}\right\}+(\sin x)^{\cos x}{/tex} {cos x {tex}\cdot{/tex} cot x - sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log sin x}
solution_html:
Let, y = xsin x + (sin x)cos x = u + v {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}}+\frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex}
u = xsin x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log u = sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d u}{d x}=x^{\sin x}\left\{\cos x \cdot \log x+\frac{\sin x}{x}\right\}{/tex}
v = (sin x)cos x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log v = cos x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (sin x) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{dv}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} = (sin x)cos x {cos x{tex}\cdot{/tex}cot x - sin x{tex}\cdot{/tex}log sin x}
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=x^{\sin x}\left\{\cos x \cdot \log x+\frac{\sin x}{x}\right\}+(\sin x)^{\cos x}{/tex} {cos x{tex}\cdot{/tex}cot x - sin x{tex}\cdot{/tex}log sin x}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If (cos x)y = (sin y)x, find {tex}\frac {dy} {dx}{/tex}.
question_text: If (cos x) y = (sin y) x , find {tex}\frac {dy} {dx}{/tex} .
explanation: (cos x) y = (sin y) x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (cos x) = x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (sin y) Differentiating w.r.t 'x' {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (cos x) + y(-tan x) = log(sin y) + x ⋅ cot y &sdot {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y \cdot \tan x+\log (\sin y)}{\log (\cos x)-x \cot y}{/tex}
solution: (cos x) y = (sin y) x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (cos x) = x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (sin y) Differentiating w.r.t 'x' {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (cos x) + y(-tan x) = log(sin y) + x ⋅ cot y &sdot {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y \cdot \tan x+\log (\sin y)}{\log (\cos x)-x \cot y}{/tex}
solution_html:
(cos x)y = (sin y)x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (cos x) = x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log (sin y)
Differentiating w.r.t 'x'
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex}{tex}\cdot{/tex} log (cos x) + y(-tan x) = log(sin y) + x ⋅ cot y &sdot {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y \cdot \tan x+\log (\sin y)}{\log (\cos x)-x \cot y}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} if sin2 x + cos2 y = 1
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} if sin 2 x + cos 2 y = 1
explanation: It is given that sin 2 x + cos 2 y = 1 Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get, {tex}\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{2} x+\cos ^{2} y\right)=\frac{d}{d x}(1){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{2} x\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(\cos ^{2} y\right)=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \sin x \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\sin x)+2 \cos y \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\cos y)=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \sin x \cos x+2 \cos y(-\sin y) \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin 2 x-\sin 2 y \frac{d y}{d x}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sin 2 x}{\sin 2 y}{/tex}
solution: It is given that sin 2 x + cos 2 y = 1 Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get, {tex}\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{2} x+\cos ^{2} y\right)=\frac{d}{d x}(1){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{2} x\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(\cos ^{2} y\right)=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \sin x \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\sin x)+2 \cos y \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\cos y)=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \sin x \cos x+2 \cos y(-\sin y) \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin 2 x-\sin 2 y \frac{d y}{d x}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sin 2 x}{\sin 2 y}{/tex}
solution_html:
It is given that sin2 x + cos2 y = 1
Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get,
{tex}\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{2} x+\cos ^{2} y\right)=\frac{d}{d x}(1){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{2} x\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(\cos ^{2} y\right)=0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \sin x \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\sin x)+2 \cos y \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\cos y)=0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \sin x \cos x+2 \cos y(-\sin y) \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}=0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \sin 2 x-\sin 2 y \frac{d y}{d x}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\sin 2 x}{\sin 2 y}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}y=2 \sqrt{\sec \left(e^{2 x}\right)}{/tex}, then find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}y=2 \sqrt{\sec \left(e^{2 x}\right)}{/tex} , then find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\sec \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}}} \cdot \sec \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}} \tan \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}} \cdot 2 \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}\sqrt{\sec \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}} \tan \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}} {/tex}
solution: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\sec \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}}} \cdot \sec \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}} \tan \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}} \cdot 2 \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}\sqrt{\sec \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}} \tan \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}} {/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\sec \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}}} \cdot \sec \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}} \tan \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}} \cdot 2 \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}{/tex}
=  {tex}2 \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}\sqrt{\sec \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}} \tan \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}} {/tex}

question_html: If y = (sin x)x + sin-1{tex}\left(\sqrt{1-x^2}\right){/tex}, then find {tex}\frac {dy} {dx}{/tex}.
question_text: If y = (sin x) x + sin -1 {tex}\left(\sqrt{1-x^2}\right){/tex} , then find {tex}\frac {dy} {dx}{/tex} .
explanation: y = (sin x) x + sin -1 {tex}\sqrt{1-x^2}{/tex} Let A = (sin x) x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log A = x log sin x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{~A}} \frac{\mathrm{dA}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} = log sin x + x cot x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{dA}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} = (sin x) x (log sin x + x cot x) B = {tex}\sin ^{-1} \sqrt{1-x^2}{/tex} = cos -1 x {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dB}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-\mathrm{x}^2}}{/tex} {tex}y=A+B \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d A}{d x}+\frac{d A}{d x}{/tex} {tex}=(\sin x)^x(\log \sin x+x \cot x)-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex}
solution: y = (sin x) x + sin -1 {tex}\sqrt{1-x^2}{/tex} Let A = (sin x) x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log A = x log sin x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{~A}} \frac{\mathrm{dA}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} = log sin x + x cot x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{dA}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} = (sin x) x (log sin x + x cot x) B = {tex}\sin ^{-1} \sqrt{1-x^2}{/tex} = cos -1 x {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dB}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-\mathrm{x}^2}}{/tex} {tex}y=A+B \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d A}{d x}+\frac{d A}{d x}{/tex} {tex}=(\sin x)^x(\log \sin x+x \cot x)-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex}
solution_html:
y = (sin x)x + sin-1 {tex}\sqrt{1-x^2}{/tex}
Let A = (sin x)x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log A = x log sin x
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{~A}} \frac{\mathrm{dA}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} = log sin x + x cot x
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{dA}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} = (sin x)x (log sin x + x cot x)
B = {tex}\sin ^{-1} \sqrt{1-x^2}{/tex} = cos-1 x
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dB}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-\mathrm{x}^2}}{/tex}
{tex}y=A+B \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d A}{d x}+\frac{d A}{d x}{/tex} {tex}=(\sin x)^x(\log \sin x+x \cot x)-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex}, if xy {tex}\cdot{/tex} yx = xx.
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} , if x y {tex}\cdot{/tex} y x = x x .
explanation: x y {tex}\cdot{/tex} y x = x x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log x + x log y = x log x differentiate both sides w.r.t. x, {tex} \left(y \cdot \frac{1}{x}+\log x \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}\right) {/tex} + {tex} \left(x \cdot \frac{1}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}+\log y \cdot 1\right) {/tex} = {tex} x \cdot \frac{1}{x} {/tex} + log x {tex}\cdot{/tex} 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} \frac{y}{x}+\log \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-1 {/tex} {tex} =-\left(\log x+\frac{x}{y}\right) \cdot \frac{d y}{d x} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1-\frac{y}{x}-\log \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{\log x+\frac{x}{y}} {/tex} or {tex} \frac{y}{x}\left[\frac{x+x \log x-y-x \log y}{y \log x+x}\right] {/tex}
solution: x y {tex}\cdot{/tex} y x = x x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log x + x log y = x log x differentiate both sides w.r.t. x, {tex} \left(y \cdot \frac{1}{x}+\log x \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}\right) {/tex} + {tex} \left(x \cdot \frac{1}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}+\log y \cdot 1\right) {/tex} = {tex} x \cdot \frac{1}{x} {/tex} + log x {tex}\cdot{/tex} 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} \frac{y}{x}+\log \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-1 {/tex} {tex} =-\left(\log x+\frac{x}{y}\right) \cdot \frac{d y}{d x} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1-\frac{y}{x}-\log \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{\log x+\frac{x}{y}} {/tex} or {tex} \frac{y}{x}\left[\frac{x+x \log x-y-x \log y}{y \log x+x}\right] {/tex}
solution_html:
xy {tex}\cdot{/tex} yx = xx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y log x + x log y = x log x
differentiate both sides w.r.t. x,
{tex} \left(y \cdot \frac{1}{x}+\log x \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}\right) {/tex} + {tex} \left(x \cdot \frac{1}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}+\log y \cdot 1\right) {/tex} = {tex} x \cdot \frac{1}{x} {/tex} + log x {tex}\cdot{/tex} 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex} \frac{y}{x}+\log \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-1 {/tex} {tex} =-\left(\log x+\frac{x}{y}\right) \cdot \frac{d y}{d x} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1-\frac{y}{x}-\log \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)}{\log x+\frac{x}{y}} {/tex} or {tex} \frac{y}{x}\left[\frac{x+x \log x-y-x \log y}{y \log x+x}\right] {/tex}
